{"id":"2f29bfac-e5c2-4808-8ba2-a1fe32b68d95","identifier":"FRG-227","title":"Bug: FALLBACK events never appear in debug event log","description":"## Problem

QA ran 14+ sim hours across two sessions. Characters DO redirect when targets are full (behavior works), but no FALLBACK event ever appears in the debug event log. The fallback event emission or consumption in debug.html is broken.

## Evidence

* Zero FALLBACK entries in 14+ hours of observation
* Characters visibly redirect (e.g., Nyx targets bonsai while Maple is using it, Nyx walks elsewhere)
* LLM even acknowledges conflicts in dialogue (Maple: 'it looks like Sol is already using the tea set')
* The code in Engine.js pushes fallback events via pendingEvents, and debug.html has CSS + rendering for them

## Investigation

1. Check if \\_fallbackToAlternativeObject is actually being called (add console.log)
2. Check if pendingEvents consumption in debug.html tick loop handles fallback events correctly
3. Verify the event type string matches between push and render

## Acceptance Criteria

* FALLBACK events visible in debug event log when character redirects
* Events show reason and from/to objects","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-227/bug-fallback-events-never-appear-in-debug-event-log","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-227-bug-fallback-events-never-appear-in-debug-event-log","createdAt":"2026-03-07T21:18:40.598Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T21:18:40.598Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

SUCCESS 
   660→              <span class="log-tag talk">TALK</span>
   661→            </div>
   662→          `;
   663→        }
   664→        if (e.type === 'talk_rejected') {
   665→          const reasonStr = e.reason ? ` (${e.reason})` : '';
   666→          return `
   667→            <div class="log-entry reject">
   668→              <span class="log-time">${e.time}</span>
   669→              <span class="log-obj">${e.initiatorLabel}</span>
   670→              <span class="log-transition">\u2192 ${e.targetLabel} rejected${reasonStr}</span>
   671→              <span class="log-tag reject">REJECT</span>
   672→            </div>
   673→          `;
   674→        }
   675→        if (e.type === 'fallback') {
   676→          const fromStr = e.fromLabel ? e.fromLabel : '';
   677→          const toStr   = e.toLabel   ? ` \u2192 ${e.toLabel}` : ' \u2192 idle';
   678→          const reason  = e.reason === 'object_unavailable' ? 'unavailable'
   679→                        : e.reason === 'talk_target_moved'  ? 'target moved'
   680→                        : e.reason ?? '';
   681→          return `
   682→            <div class="log-entry fallback">
   683→              <span class="log-time">${e.time}</span>
   684→              <span class="log-obj">${e.charLabel}</span>
   685→              <span class="log-transition">${fromStr} ${reason}${toStr}</span>
   686→              <span class="log-tag fallback">FALLBACK</span>
   687→            </div>
   688→          `;
   689→        }
   690→        return `
   691→          <div class="log-entry ${e.trigger === 'auto' ? 'auto' : 'interaction'}">
   692→            <span class="log-time">${e.time}</span>
   693→            <span class="log-obj">${e.objectId}</span>
   694→            <span class="log-transition">${e.fromState} &rarr; ${e.toState}</span>
   695→            <span class="log-tag ${e.trigger === 'auto' ? 'auto' : 'use'}">${e.trigger}</span>
   696→          </div>
   697→        `;
   698→      }).join('');
   699→    }
   700→
   701→    // ── Tick loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   702→
   703→    function tick(ts) {
   704→      requestAnimationFrame(tick);
   705→
   706→      if (!paused && lastTs !== null) {
   707→        const realDelta = (ts - lastTs) * speed;
   708→
   709→        // Snapshot states before update
   710→        const snap = new Map();
   711→        for (const obj of simEngine.objectEngine.getAll()) snap.set(obj.id, obj.state);
   712→
   713→        // Advance simulation
   714→        simEngine.update(realDelta);
   715→
   716→        // Detect auto-transitions
   717→        for (const obj of simEngine.objectEngine.getAll()) {
   718→          if (snap.get(obj.id) !== obj.state) {
   719→            pushLog(obj.id, snap.get(obj.id), obj.state, 'auto');
   720→            prevStateMap.set(obj.id, obj.state);
   721→          }
   722→          updateObjectCard(obj);
   723→        }
   724→
   725→        // Consume sim events: talk, fallback, emoji
   726→        while (simEngine.pendingEvents.length > 0) {
   727→          const ev = simEngine.pendingEvents.shift();
   728→          if (ev.type === 'conversation_start' || ev.type === 'talk_rejected' || ev.type === 'fallback') {
   729→            log.unshift({ ...ev, time: simEngine.getSimTimeString() });
   730→            if (log.length > MAX_LOG) log.length = MAX_LOG;
   731→            logDirty = true;
   732→          }
   733→          // emoji events: no-op for debug (scene.js handles floating emojis)
   734→        }
   735→
   736→        // Update character cards
   737→        simEngine.characters.forEach((char, i) => updateCharacterCard(char, i));
   738→      }
   739→
   740→      lastTs = ts;
   741→
   742→      // Clock
   743→      document.getElementById('clock').textContent = simEngine.getSimTimeString();
   744→
   745→      // Event log (only when dirty)
   746→      renderEventLog();
   747→    }
   748→
   749→    // ── Init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   750→
   751→    initObjectCards();
   752→    initCharacterCards();
   753→    requestAnimationFrame(tick);
   754→  </script>
   755→</body>
   756→</html>
   757→

USERMESSAGE #20
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   130→    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);
   131→
   132→    if (event) {
   133→      // Convert reservation → active occupancy
   134→      this.objectEngine.arrive(objectId, char.name);
   135→
   136→      const obj = this.objectEngine.getById(objectId);
   137→
   138→      // Hold the character for the full auto_next duration if the new state has one;
   139→      // otherwise fall back to the object's generic interactMs.
   140→      let holdMs = obj ? obj.interactMs : 8000;
   141→      if (obj && event.state) {
   142→        const stateDef = obj.states[event.state];
   143→        if (stateDef?.auto_next?.after) {
   144→          holdMs = stateDef.auto_next.after * 1000;
   145→        }
   146→      }
   147→
   148→      char.startInteraction(objectId, holdMs);
   149→
   150→      if (event.speech) char.pendingSpeech = event.speech;
   151→      if (event.emoji)  this.pendingEvents.push(event);
   152→    } else {
   153→      // No valid transition (object changed state mid-walk) — release the
   154→      // reservation and try to find an alternative object.
   155→      this.objectEngine.release(objectId, char.name);
   156→      this._fallbackToAlternativeObject(char, objectId);
   157→    }
   158→  }
   159→
   160→  // ── Talk handshake ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   161→
   162→  /**
   163→   * Called when a character arrives at the tile adjacent to their talk target.
   164→   * Checks proximity and re-evaluates B's availability before starting conversation.
   165→   */
   166→  _handleTalkArrival(char) {
   167→    const target = char.pendingTalkTarget;
   168→    char.pendingTalkTarget = null;
   169→
   170→    // Proximity check: target may have moved significantly during the walk.
   171→    const dx   = Math.round(target.tx) - Math.round(char.tx);
   172→    const dy   = Math.round(target.ty) - Math.round(char.ty);
   173→    const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
   174→
   175→    if (dist > 3) {
   176→      // Target has drifted — try to follow if not too far
   177→      if (dist <= 10) {
   178→        const adjTile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty));
   179→        if (adjTile && char.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {
   180→          // Re-attach pending talk so we retry on next arrival
   181→          char.pendingTalkTarget   = target;
   182→          char._talkRetargetTimer  = 2000;
   183→          return;
   184→        }
   185→      }
   186→      // Too far or unreachable — abort
   187→      this.pendingEvents.push({
   188→        type:      'fallback',
   189→        charLabel: char.label,
   190→        reason:    'talk_target_moved',
   191→        fromLabel: target.label,
   192→        toLabel:   null,
   193→      });
   194→      char.pendingTalkDialogue = null;
   195→      char.pendingTalkResponse = null;
   196→      char.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   197→      return;
   198→    }
   199→
   200→    // Re-check availability: accept if idle or walking, reject if busy
   201→    const canTalk = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';
   202→
   203→    if (!canTalk) {
   204→      this.pendingEvents.push({
   205→        type:           'talk_rejected',
   206→        initiatorLabel: char.label,
   207→        targetLabel:    target.label,
   208→      });
   209→      char.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   210→      return;
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    this._startConversation(char, target);
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  /**
   217→   * Configurable conversation duration range (ms).
   218→   * Defaults to 10–15 s; override via constructor config.
   219→   */

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   340→  }
   341→
   342→  /**
   343→   * Called when a character's object interaction fails on arrival.
   344→   * Tries to redirect to the nearest available alternative; emits a 'fallback' event.
   345→   */
   346→  _fallbackToAlternativeObject(char, fromId) {
   347→    const fromObj     = this.objectEngine.getById(fromId);
   348→    const alternative = this._findAlternativeObject(char, fromId);
   349→
   350→    if (alternative) {
   351→      const adjTile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(alternative.tx, alternative.ty);
   352→      if (adjTile && char.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {
   353→        alternative.reserve(char.name);
   354→        char.targetObjectId = alternative.id;
   355→        char.pendingAction  = 'use';
   356→
   357→        this.pendingEvents.push({
   358→          type:      'fallback',
   359→          charLabel: char.label,
   360→          reason:    'object_unavailable',
   361→          fromLabel: fromObj?.name ?? fromId,
   362→          toLabel:   alternative.name,
   363→        });
   364→        return;
   365→      }
   366→    }
   367→
   368→    // No usable alternative — idle
   369→    char.wanderTimer = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   370→    this.pendingEvents.push({
   371→      type:      'fallback',
   372→      charLabel: char.label,
   373→      reason:    'object_unavailable',
   374→      fromLabel: fromObj?.name ?? fromId,
   375→      toLabel:   null,
   376→    });
   377→  }
   378→
   379→  /**
   380→   * Called periodically while a character is walking toward a talk target.
   381→   * Re-routes the character if the target has moved, or aborts if too far.
   382→   */
   383→  _updateTalkRoute(char) {
   384→    const target = char.pendingTalkTarget;
   385→    if (!target) return;
   386→
   387→    const dx   = Math.round(target.tx) - Math.round(char.tx);
   388→    const dy   = Math.round(target.ty) - Math.round(char.ty);
   389→    const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
   390→
   391→    // If target has wandered more than 10 tiles away, give up
   392→    if (dist > 10) {
   393→      char.pendingTalkTarget   = null;
   394→      char.pendingTalkDialogue = null;
   395→      char.pendingTalkResponse = null;
   396→      char.path                = [];
   397→      char.action              = 'idle';
   398→      char.wanderTimer         = 3000 + Math.random() * 5000;
   399→
   400→      this.pendingEvents.push({
   401→        type:      'fallback',
   402→        charLabel: char.label,
   403→        reason:    'talk_target_moved',
   404→        fromLabel: target.label,
   405→        toLabel:   null,
   406→      });
   407→      return;
   408→    }
   409→
   410→    // Re-route only if the path end has drifted more than 1 tile from target
   411→    const pathEnd = char.path[char.path.length - 1];
   412→    if (pathEnd) {
   413→      const edx = pathEnd.tx - Math.round(target.tx);
   414→      const edy = pathEnd.ty - Math.round(target.ty);
   415→      if (Math.sqrt(edx * edx + edy * edy) > 1) {
   416→        const adjTile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty));
   417→        if (adjTile) char.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty);
   418→      }
   419→    }

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   240→      charB.facing = dy >= 0 ? 'up'   : 'down';
   241→    }
   242→
   243→    // Transfer queued dialogue to speech bubbles
   244→    if (charA.pendingTalkDialogue) {
   245→      charA.pendingSpeech          = charA.pendingTalkDialogue;
   246→      charA.pendingTalkDialogue    = null;
   247→    }
   248→    if (charA.pendingTalkResponse) {
   249→      charB.pendingSpeech          = charA.pendingTalkResponse;
   250→      charA.pendingTalkResponse    = null;
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    charA.startTalk(charB, duration);
   254→    charB.startTalk(charA, duration);
   255→
   256→    this.pendingEvents.push({
   257→      type:           'conversation_start',
   258→      initiatorLabel: charA.label,
   259→      targetLabel:    charB.label,
   260→    });
   261→  }
   262→
   263→  // ── Wander ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   264→
   265→  _doWander(char) {
   266→    // 30% chance: walk to a random interactive object instead of wandering freely
   267→    if (Math.random() < 0.3) {
   268→      const objects = this.objectEngine.getAll();
   269→      if (objects.length > 0) {
   270→        // Shuffle and try each until one has an available slot and a reachable adjacent tile
   271→        const shuffled = objects.slice().sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5);
   272→        for (const obj of shuffled) {
   273→          if (obj.isFull()) continue; // skip objects at capacity
   274→          // Only target objects whose current state accepts a 'use' interaction
   275→          const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   276→          if (!stateDef?.transitions?.use) continue;
   277→          const tile = this._findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty);
   278→          if (tile && char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty)) {
   279→            obj.reserve(char.name); // claim a slot before walking
   280→            char.targetObjectId = obj.id;
   281→            char.pendingAction  = 'use';
   282→            return;
   283→          }
   284→        }
   285→        // All objects full/non-interactable/unreachable — fall through to random wander
   286→      }
   287→    }
   288→
   289→    // Random walkable tile
   290→    const tiles = this._getWalkableTiles();
   291→    if (tiles.length === 0) {
   292→      char.wanderTimer = 2000;
   293→      return;
   294→    }
   295→
   296→    const cx = Math.round(char.tx);
   297→    const cy = Math.round(char.ty);
   298→
   299→    let moved = false;
   300→    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 15; attempt++) {
   301→      const tile = tiles[Math.floor(Math.random() * tiles.length)];
   302→      if (tile.tx === cx && tile.ty === cy) continue;
   303→      moved = char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty);
   304→      if (moved) break;
   305→    }
   306→
   307→    if (!moved) {
   308→      char.wanderTimer = 2000;
   309→    }
   310→  }
   311→
   312→  // ── Fallback helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   313→
   314→  /**
   315→   * Find the closest available object that `char` can interact with right now,
   316→   * excluding the object with `excludeId`.
   317→   * @returns {ObjectInstance|null}
   318→   */
   319→  _findAlternativeObject(char, excludeId) {
   320→    const objects = this.objectEngine.getAll();
   321→    let best     = null;
   322→    let bestDist = Infinity;
   323→
   324→    for (const obj of objects) {
   325→      if (obj.id === excludeId) continue;
   326→      if (obj.isFull()) continue;
   327→      // Require a usable state right now
   328→      const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   329→      if (!stateDef?.transitions?.use) continue;
   330→      // Require a reachable adjacent tile
   331→      if (!this._findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty)) continue;
   332→
   333→      const dx   = obj.tx - Math.round(char.tx);
   334→      const dy   = obj.ty - Math.round(char.ty);
   335→      const dist = dx * dx + dy * dy;
   336→      if (dist < bestDist) { bestDist = dist; best = obj; }
   337→    }
   338→
   339→    return best;

// claim a slot before walking\n   280\u2192            char.targetObjectId = obj.id;\n   281\u2192            char.pendingAction  = 'use';\n   282\u2192            return;\n   283\u2192          }\n   284\u2192        }\n   285\u2192        // All objects full/non-interactable/unreachable \u2014 fall through to random wander\n   286\u2192      }\n   287\u2192    }\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192    // Random walkable tile\n   290\u2192    const tiles = this._getWalkableTiles();\n   291\u2192    if (tiles.length === 0) {\n   292\u2192      char.wanderTimer = 2000;\n   293\u2192      return;\n   294\u2192    }\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192    const cx = Math.round(char.tx);\n   297\u2192    const cy = Math.round(char.ty);\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192    let moved = false;\n   300\u2192    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 15; attempt++) {\n   301\u2192      const tile = tiles[Math.floor(Math.random() * tiles.length)];\n   302\u2192      if (tile.tx === cx && tile.ty === cy) continue;\n   303\u2192      moved = char.moveTo(tile.tx, tile.ty);\n   304\u2192      if (moved) break;\n   305\u2192    }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192    if (!moved) {\n   308\u2192      char.wanderTimer = 2000;\n   309\u2192    }\n   310\u2192  }\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Fallback helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192  /**\n   315\u2192   * Find the closest available object that `char` can interact with right now,\n   316\u2192   * excluding the object with `excludeId`.\n   317\u2192   * @returns {ObjectInstance|null}\n   318\u2192   */\n   319\u2192  _findAlternativeObject(char, excludeId) {\n   320\u2192    const objects = this.objectEngine.getAll();\n   321\u2192    let best     = null;\n   322\u2192    let bestDist = Infinity;\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192    for (const obj of objects) {\n   325\u2192      if (obj.id === excludeId) continue;\n   326\u2192      if (obj.isFull()) continue;\n   327\u2192      // Require a usable state right now\n   328\u2192      const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];\n   329\u2192      if (!stateDef?.transitions?.use) continue;\n   330\u2192      // Require a reachable adjacent tile\n   331\u2192      if (!this._findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty)) continue;\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192      const dx   = obj.tx - Math.round(char.tx);\n   334\u2192      const dy   = obj.ty - Math.round(char.ty);\n   335\u2192      const dist = dx * dx + dy * dy;\n   336\u2192      if (dist < bestDist) { bestDist = dist; ASSISTANTMESSAGE #26
Now I understand the structure. Let me look at the full Engine.js to understand where `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` is called from, especially when there's a "slot full" scenario, and what happens before the character "arrives" at the destination. The key issue the bug report mentions is: "Characters DO redirect when targets are full (behavior works), but no FALLBACK event ever appears in the debug event log." So the fallback function IS being called (character redirects), but the events don't appear in the debug log. Let me look at how the debug.html consumes events more carefully. At line 726-734: I'm checking the event filtering logic in the debug display—it's pulling from `pendingEvents` and only logging conversation_start, talk_rejected, and fallback events, which looks correct. Now I'm tracing through where fallback events actually get emitted. The `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` function is called from `_handleObjectInteract` when the interact call returns null, but I need to understand the full sequence—specifically when a character arrives at an object that's already occupied by another character and how that triggers the fallback path. Looking at the reservation system, characters claim a slot before they even start walking to an object, so the issue isn't about arriving and finding it taken. The code shows that when a character targets an object, it reserves the slot immediately, and if an alternative is needed, it reserves that one too and pushes a pending event. Now I need to trace through the Engine.js update flow to see if these pending events are being cleared somewhere before the debug interface can display them, or if the fallback event is firing at an unexpected time.

The code shows that when a character targets an object, it reserves the slot immediately, and if an alternative is needed, it reserves that one too and pushes a pending event.\n\nNow I need to trace through the Engine.js update flow to see if these pending events are being cleared somewhere before the debug interface can display them, or if the fallback event is firing at an unexpected time.",

SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * Engine.js — SimulationEngine: manages characters, object state machines, and
     3→ * default random-wander behaviour (with 30% chance of object interaction).
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { ObjectEngine, OBJECT_DEFS } from './objects.js';
     7→
     8→export class SimulationEngine {
     9→  /**
    10→   * @param {object}      config
    11→   * @param {boolean[][]} config.walkable   - walkable[row][col]
    12→   * @param {number}      config.gridCols
    13→   * @param {number}      config.gridRows
    14→   * @param {object}      [config.aiEngine] - AIEngine instance (optional)
    15→   */
    16→  constructor(config = {}) {
    17→    this.walkable  = config.walkable  || [];
    18→    this.gridCols  = config.gridCols  || 19;
    19→    this.gridRows  = config.gridRows  || 13;
    20→    this.aiEngine  = config.aiEngine  || null;
    21→
    22→    this.characters = [];
    23→
    24→    // Object state machine engine — pre-loaded with Japanese Home objects
    25→    this.objectEngine = new ObjectEngine();
    26→    this.objectEngine.addObjects(OBJECT_DEFS);
    27→
    28→    // Events queued for scene to consume (emoji floats, etc.)
    29→    this.pendingEvents = [];
    30→
    31→    // Sim clock — starts at 08:00 AM game-time
    32→    this.simTimeMs = 8 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    33→    // 1 real second = 60 sim seconds  (1 real min = 1 sim hour)
    34→    this.simSpeed  = 60;
    35→
    36→    // Cache walkable tiles list (built lazily)
    37→    this._walkableTiles = null;
    38→  }
    39→
    40→  addCharacter(char) {
    41→    this.characters.push(char);
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  // ── Main update loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→
    46→  update(deltaMs) {
    47→    // Advance sim clock
    48→    this.simTimeMs += deltaMs * this.simSpeed;
    49→
    50→    // Optional AI decisions — pass live object list so AI sees current states
    51→    if (this.aiEngine) {
    52→      this.aiEngine.update(deltaMs, this.characters, this.objectEngine.getAll(), {
    53→        timeString: this.getSimTimeString(),
    54→        findAdjacentWalkable: this._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(this),
    55→        pushEvent: (ev) => this.pendingEvents.push(ev),
    56→      });
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    // Tick object state machines; collect auto-transition events
    60→    const objEvents = this.objectEngine.tick(deltaMs);
    61→    for (const ev of objEvents) {
    62→      if (ev.emoji) this.pendingEvents.push(ev);
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    for (const char of this.characters) {
    66→      // Save pre-update state to detect interaction-end transitions
    67→      const prevAction         = char.action;
    68→      const prevInteractTarget = char.interactTarget;
    69→
    70→      // Handle arrival: character reached target object tile
    71→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char.targetObjectId) {
    72→        this._handleObjectArrival(char);
    73→      }
    74→
    75→      // Handle arrival: character reached talk target
    76→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char.pendingTalkTarget) {
    77→        this._handleTalkArrival(char);
    78→      }
    79→
    80→      // Mid-walk: periodically re-route toward a moving talk target
    81→      if (char.action === 'walking' && char.pendingTalkTarget) {
    82→        char._talkRetargetTimer -= deltaMs;
    83→        if (char._talkRetargetTimer <= 0) {
    84→          char._talkRetargetTimer = 2000; // re-check every 2 s
    85→          this._updateTalkRoute(char);
    86→        }
    87→      }
    88→
    89→      // Trigger random wander (or object visit) when idle and timer expired
    90→      if (char.action === 'idle' && char.wanderTimer <= 0) {
    91→        this._doWander(char);
    92→      }
    93→
    94→      // Tick speech timer — fire a random line when it expires
    95→      if (char.dialogue.length > 0 && char.pendingSpeech === null) {
    96→        char.speechTimer -= deltaMs;
    97→        if (char.speechTimer <= 0) {
    98→          const idx = Math.floor(Math.random() * char.dialogue.length);
    99→          char.pendingSpeech = char.dialogue[idx];
   100→          char.speechTimer   = 20000 + Math.random() * 20000; // reset 20–40 s
   101→        }
   102→      }
   103→
   104→      // Per-character update (movement, animations, sprite sync)
   105→      char.update(deltaMs);
   106→
   107→      // Detect interaction end: release slot so other characters can use the object
   108→      if (prevAction === 'interacting' && char.action !== 'interacting' && prevInteractTarget) {
   109→        this.objectEngine.release(prevInteractTarget, char.name);
   110→      }
   111→    }
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  // ── Object interaction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   115→
   116→  /**
   117→   * Called when a character arrives at an object's adjacent tile.
   118→   * Fires the 'use' transition and puts the character into 'interacting' state.
   119→   * If the object has no valid transition, finds an alternative instead of idling.
   120→   */
   121→  _handleObjectArrival(char) {
   122→    const objectId = char.targetObjectId;
   123→    const action   = char.pendingAction || 'use';
   124→
   125→    // Clear targeting immediately so we don't re-fire on the next idle frame.
   126→    // _fallbackToAlternativeObject may set a new targetObjectId if it finds one.
   127→    char.targetObjectId = null;
   128→    char.pendingAction  = null;
   129→
   130→    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #29
Now let me understand the flow: 1. When a character arrives at their target object tile, `_handleObjectArrival` is called. 2. This calls `objectEngine.interact()` which fires a transition. 3. If `interact()` returns null (no valid transition), it calls `_fallbackToAlternativeObject`. But the issue is: Why would `interact()` return null when the character already reserved the slot before walking to it? Let me look at the `objects.js` to understand the `interact()` method and the reservation system. Actually wait - let me re-read the issue more carefully. The issue says: - Characters ARE redirecting when targets are full, so the behavior works - But no FALLBACK event ever shows up in the debug event log So the redirect is happening, but the fallback event isn't appearing. The events should be getting pushed to `pendingEvents` based on the code, and debug.html should be consuming them in its tick loop. Let me check what's happening with the event consumption... Checking the debug event loop... Actually, I'm wondering if there are two separate engine instances here. The character redirects might be happening through AIEngine rather than through the Engine.js fallback mechanism. Let me trace through AIEngine to see how it's handling redirects.

     1→<!DOCTYPE html>
     2→<html lang="en">
     3→<head>
     4→  <meta charset="UTF-8">
     5→  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
     6→  <title>AI Aquarium — Debug Dashboard</title>
     7→  <style>
     8→    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
     9→
    10→    body {
    11→      background: #1a1a2e;
    12→      color: #e0ddd4;
    13→      font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace;
    14→      font-size: 13px;
    15→      min-height: 100vh;
    16→      padding: 16px;
    17→    }
    18→
    19→    /* ── Header ── */
    20→    #header {
    21→      display: flex;
    22→      align-items: center;
    23→      justify-content: space-between;
    24→      border-bottom: 1px solid #333;
    25→      padding-bottom: 12px;
    26→      margin-bottom: 16px;
    27→    }
    28→    #header h1 { font-size: 18px; color: #ffe8a3; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }
    29→    #clock { font-size: 22px; color: #ffe8a3; min-width: 110px; text-align: right; }
    30→
    31→    /* ── Controls ── */
    32→    #controls {
    33→      display: flex;
    34→      gap: 8px;
    35→      margin-bottom: 20px;
    36→      align-items: center;
    37→      flex-wrap: wrap;
    38→    }
    39→    button {
    40→      padding: 5px 12px;
    41→      background: #222240;
    42→      color: #e0ddd4;
    43→      border: 1px solid #444;
    44→      border-radius: 3px;
    45→      cursor: pointer;
    46→      font-family: inherit;
    47→      font-size: 12px;
    48→      transition: background 0.1s;
    49→    }
    50→    button:hover { background: #2a2a5a; }
    51→    button.active { background: #2a3a5a; border-color: #6a9a8a; color: #9eebd4; }
    52→    .speed-btn.active { background: #3a2a5a; border-color: #8a6aaa; color: #c8a0ee; }
    53→    .ctrl-sep { width: 1px; height: 24px; background: #333; margin: 0 4px; }
    54→    .ctrl-label { color: #888; font-size: 11px; }
    55→
    56→    /* ── Layout ── */
    57→    .section-title {
    58→      font-size: 11px;
    59→      color: #777;
    60→      text-transform: uppercase;
    61→      letter-spacing: 0.1em;
    62→      margin-bottom: 10px;
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    .main-grid {
    66→      display: grid;
    67→      grid-template-columns: 1fr 300px;
    68→      gap: 16px;
    69→    }
    70→    @media (max-width: 900px) { .main-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }
    71→
    72→    .left-col { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 20px; }
    73→
    74→    /* ── Object cards ── */
    75→    #objects-grid {
    76→      display: grid;
    77→      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(210px, 1fr));
    78→      gap: 8px;
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    .object-card {
    82→      background: #222240;
    83→      border: 1px solid #333;
    84→      border-radius: 6px;
    85→      padding: 10px 14px;
    86→    }
    87→    .object-card-header {
    88→      display: flex;
    89→      align-items: center;
    90→      justify-content: space-between;
    91→      margin-bottom: 4px;
    92→    }
    93→    .object-name { font-weight: bold; color: #c8d4e8; text-transform: capitalize; }
    94→
    95→    .state-badge {
    96→      padding: 2px 8px;
    97→      border-radius: 3px;
    98→      font-size: 11px;
    99→    }
   100→    .state-badge.idle   { background: #1a2a1a; color: #77cc77; border: 1px solid #2a4a2a; }

USERMESSAGE #33
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→/**
     2→ * AIEngine.js — LLM-driven decision engine.
     3→ *
     4→ * When `endpoint` is null (default) the engine is disabled and characters
     5→ * fall back to random wander controlled by SimulationEngine.
     6→ *
     7→ * To enable, pass an endpoint URL and optionally an API key:
     8→ *   new AIEngine({ endpoint: 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', apiKey: '...' })
     9→ *
    10→ * The engine polls each character independently on a configurable interval.
    11→ * Responses are expected as JSON with this shape:
    12→ *   { "action": "move|interact|idle|talk",
    13→ *     "targetId": "object_id",               // for interact
    14→ *     "targetTile": { "tx": 5, "ty": 9 },    // for move
    15→ *     "targetCharacter": "character_name",   // for talk
    16→ *     "dialogue": "optional speech",
    17→ *     "targetDialogue": "response from target character", // for talk
    18→ *     "description": "what the character is doing" }
    19→ */
    20→
    21→export class AIEngine {
    22→  /**
    23→   * @param {object}  [config]
    24→   * @param {string}  [config.endpoint]            - full URL of LLM API endpoint
    25→   * @param {string}  [config.apiKey]              - bearer token / API key
    26→   * @param {string}  [config.model]               - model ID (for Anthropic-style APIs)
    27→   * @param {number}  [config.decisionIntervalMs]  - ms between decisions per char (default 30 000)
    28→   */
    29→  constructor(config = {}) {
    30→    this.endpoint           = config.endpoint           || null;
    31→    this.apiKey             = config.apiKey             || null;
    32→    this.model              = config.model              || 'claude-sonnet-4-6';
    33→    this.decisionIntervalMs = config.decisionIntervalMs || 30_000;
    34→    this.onDecision         = config.onDecision         || null;
    35→
    36→    this.enabled = !!this.endpoint;
    37→
    38→    // Per-character countdown timers  { name → ms until next decision }
    39→    this._timers = new Map();
    40→    // Prevent overlapping requests  { name → true }
    41→    this._pending = new Set();
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  // ── Called by SimulationEngine each frame ─────────────────────────────────
    45→
    46→  update(deltaMs, characters, objects, simState) {
    47→    if (!this.enabled) return;
    48→
    49→    for (const char of characters) {
    50→      let t = this._timers.get(char.name) ?? 0;
    51→      t -= deltaMs;
    52→
    53→      // Don't make decisions while character is held at an object or in conversation;
    54→      // let the timer keep ticking so the cooldown UI stays accurate.
    55→      if (t <= 0 && !this._pending.has(char.name) && char.action !== 'interacting' && char.action !== 'talking') {
    56→        this._timers.set(char.name, this.decisionIntervalMs);
    57→        this._requestDecision(char, characters, objects, simState);
    58→      } else {
    59→        this._timers.set(char.name, t);
    60→      }
    61→    }
    62→  }
    63→
    64→  // ── Internal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65→
    66→  async _requestDecision(character, characters, objects, simState) {
    67→    this._pending.add(character.name);
    68→
    69→    try {
    70→      const nearbyObjects = objects
    71→        .filter(obj => {
    72→          const dx = obj.tx - character.tx;
    73→          const dy = obj.ty - character.ty;
    74→          return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) <= 8;
    75→        })
    76→        .map(o => {
    77→          const slot = o.getSlotInfo();
    78→          // Resolve reserved/occupied character names → display labels for LLM context
    79→          const reservedBy = (o.getReservations?.() ?? [])
    80→            .map(n => characters.find(c => c.name === n)?.label ?? n);
    81→          const occupiedBy = (o.getOccupants?.() ?? [])
    82→            .map(n => characters.find(c => c.name === n)?.label ?? n);
    83→          return {
    84→            id:         o.id,
    85→            label:      o.label,
    86→            state:      o.state,
    87→            slots:      `${slot.available}/${slot.capacity} available`,
    88→            available:  slot.available > 0,
    89→            // Blackboard: show who is already heading here or using it
    90→            intendedBy: reservedBy,
    91→            usedBy:     occupiedBy,
    92→          };
    93→        });
    94→
    95→      const nearbyCharacters = characters
    96→        .filter(c => {
    97→          if (c.name === character.name) return false;
    98→          const dx = c.tx - character.tx;
    99→          const dy = c.ty - character.ty;
   100→          return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) <= 8;
   101→        })
   102→        .map(c => ({ label: c.label, tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty), action: c.action }));
   103→
   104→      const systemPrompt =
   105→        `You are ${character.label}, a character in a cosy Japanese home simulation. ` +
   106→        `Decide what to do next based on your mood (${character.mood}/100) and energy (${character.energy}/100). ` +
   107→        `Reply ONLY with valid JSON — no markdown fences, no extra text.`;
   108→
   109→      const userPrompt =
   110→        `Sim time: ${simState.timeString}. ` +
   111→        `You are at tile (${Math.round(character.tx)}, ${Math.round(character.ty)}). ` +
   112→        `Current action: ${character.action}. ` +
   113→        `Nearby objects: ${JSON.stringify(nearbyObjects)}. ` +
   114→        `Nearby characters: ${JSON.stringify(nearbyCharacters)}. ` +
   115→        `Choose one action:
` +
   116→        `  { "action": "move",     "targetTile": {"tx": N, "ty": N}, "dialogue": "...", "description": "..." }
` +
   117→        `  { "action": "interact", "targetId": "object_id",          "dialogue": "...", "description": "..." }
` +
   118→        `  { "action": "idle",                                        "dialogue": "...", "description": "..." }
` +
   119→        `  { "action": "talk",     "targetCharacter": "character_label", "dialogue": "what you say", "targetDialogue": "their response", "description": "..." }`;
   120→
   121→      const headers = { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' };
   122→
   123→      // Build body — compatible with Anthropic Messages API shape by default;
   124→      // swap for OpenAI chat completions if needed by changing endpoint.
   125→      const body = JSON.stringify({
   126→        model: this.model,
   127→        max_tokens: 256,
   128→        system: systemPrompt,
   129→        messages: [{ role: 'user', content: userPrompt }],
   130→      });
   131→
   132→      const res = await fetch(this.endpoint, { method: 'POST', headers, body });
   133→
   134→      if (!res.ok) {
   135→        console.warn(`[AIEngine] ${character.name}: HTTP ${res.status}`);
   136→        return;
   137→      }
   138→
   139→      const data = await res.json();
   140→
   141→      // Extract text from Anthropic-style response; fall back to OpenAI style
   142→      let text = data?.content?.[0]?.text     // Anthropic
   143→              ?? data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content // OpenAI
   144→              ?? null;
   145→
   146→      if (!text) {
   147→        console.warn(`[AIEngine] ${character.name}: unrecognised response shape`, data);
   148→        return;
   149→      }
   150→
   151→      const decision = JSON.parse(text);
   152→      this._applyDecision(character, decision, characters, objects, simState);
   153→    } catch (err) {
   154→      console.warn(`[AIEngine] ${character.name}:`, err);
   155→    } finally {
   156→      this._pending.delete(character.name);
   157→    }
   158→  }
   159→
   160→  _applyDecision(character, decision, characters, objects, simState) {
   161→    if (!decision?.action) return;
   162→
   163→    switch (decision.action) {
   164→      case 'move':
   165→        if (decision.targetTile) {
   166→          // Release any pending object reservation before moving freely
   167→          if (character.targetObjectId) {
   168→            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);
   169→            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);
   170→            character.targetObjectId = null;
   171→          }
   172→          character.moveTo(decision.targetTile.tx, decision.targetTile.ty);
   173→        }
   174→        break;
   175→
   176→      case 'interact': {
   177→        const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId);
   178→        if (obj) {
   179→          // If already heading to a different object, release that reservation first
   180→          if (character.targetObjectId && character.targetObjectId !== obj.id) {
   181→            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);
   182→            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);
   183→            character.targetObjectId = null;
   184→          }
   185→
   186→          // Only commit if a slot is available and object is currently interactable
   187→          const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   188→          if (!obj.isFull() && stateDef?.transitions?.use) {
   189→            // Walk to an adjacent walkable tile (same pattern as wander engine)
   190→            const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(obj.tx, obj.ty);
   191→            if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {
   192→              if (obj.reserve(character.name)) { // claim slot; guard against any race
   193→                character.targetObjectId = obj.id;
   194→                character.pendingAction  = 'use';
   195→              }
   196→            }
   197→          }
   198→        }
   199→        break;
   200→      }
   201→
   202→      case 'idle':
   203→        // Release any pending reservation, then stay put for one full decision interval
   204→        if (character.targetObjectId) {
   205→          const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);
   206→          if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);
   207→          character.targetObjectId = null;
   208→        }
   209→        character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;
   210→        break;
   211→
   212→      case 'talk': {
   213→        const target = characters.find(c => c.label === decision.targetCharacter);
   214→        if (target) {
   215→          // Evaluate Phase: check if B can accept a conversation request.
   216→          // Accept if idle or walking; reject if interacting, talking, or otherwise busy.
   217→          const canAccept = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';
   218→
   219→          if (!canAccept) {
   220→            // B is busy — log the rejection and let A fall back to idle/wander
   221→            if (simState?.pushEvent) {
   222→              simState.pushEvent({
   223→                type:           'talk_rejected',
   224→                initiatorLabel: character.label,
   225→                targetLabel:    target.label,
   226→                reason:         target.action,
   227→              });
   228→            }
   229→            // A idles for the normal decision interval (fallback handled by Engine wander)
   230→            character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;
   231→            break;
   232→          }
   233→
   234→          // Release any previous object reservation before walking to talk
   235→          if (character.targetObjectId) {
   236→            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);
   237→            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);
   238→            character.targetObjectId = null;
   239→          }
   240→
   241→          // Request Phase: walk A to a tile adjacent to B.
   242→          // Arrival handler in Engine will start the shared talking state.
   243→          const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty));
   244→          if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {
   245→            // Store pending talk so Engine knows what to do on arrival
   246→            character.pendingTalkTarget   = target;
   247→            character.pendingTalkDialogue = decision.dialogue       || null;
   248→            character.pendingTalkResponse = decision.targetDialogue || null;
   249→          }
   250→        }
   251→        break;
   252→      }
   253→    }
   254→
   255→    // For talk actions, dialogue is held until both characters enter talking state.
   256→    if (decision.dialogue && decision.action !== 'talk') {
   257→      character.currentDialogue = decision.dialogue;
   258→      character.dialogueTimer   = 5000;
   259→    }
   260→
   261→    if (decision.description) {
   262→      console.log(`[${character.label}] ${decision.description}`);
   263→    }
   264→
   265→    if (this.onDecision) {
   266→      this.onDecision(character, decision, objects, simState);
   267→    }
   268→  }
   269→}
   270→

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01WtCkmvC1JofvB7ckTz6eXN",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192 * AIEngine.js \u2014 LLM-driven decision engine.\n     3\u2192 *\n     4\u2192 * When `endpoint` is null (default) the engine is disabled and characters\n     5\u2192 * fall back to random wander controlled by SimulationEngine.\n     6\u2192 *\n     7\u2192 * To enable, pass an endpoint URL and optionally an API key:\n     8\u2192 *   new AIEngine({ endpoint: 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', apiKey: '...' })\n     9\u2192 *\n    10\u2192 * The engine polls each character independently on a configurable interval.\n    11\u2192 * Responses are expected as JSON with this shape:\n    12\u2192 *   { \"action\": \"move|interact|idle|talk\",\n    13\u2192 *     \"targetId\": \"object_id\",               // for interact\n    14\u2192 *     \"targetTile\": { \"tx\": 5, \"ty\": 9 },    // for move\n    15\u2192 *     \"targetCharacter\": \"character_name\",   // for talk\n    16\u2192 *     \"dialogue\": \"optional speech\",\n    17\u2192 *     \"targetDialogue\": \"response from target character\", // for talk\n    18\u2192 *     \"description\": \"what the character is doing\" }\n    19\u2192 */\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192export class AIEngine {\n    22\u2192  /**\n    23\u2192   * @param {object}  [config]\n    24\u2192   * @param {string}  [config.endpoint]            - full URL of LLM API endpoint\n    25\u2192   * @param {string}  [config.apiKey]              - bearer token / API key\n    26\u2192   * @param {string}  [config.model]               - model ID (for Anthropic-style APIs)\n    27\u2192   * @param {number}  [config.decisionIntervalMs]  - ms between decisions per char (default 30 000)\n    28\u2192   */\n    29\u2192  constructor(config = {}) {\n    30\u2192    this.endpoint           = config.endpoint           || null;\n    31\u2192    this.apiKey             = config.apiKey             || null;\n    32\u2192    this.model              = config.model              || 'claude-sonnet-4-6';\n    33\u2192    this.decisionIntervalMs = config.decisionIntervalMs || 30_000;\n    34\u2192    this.onDecision         = config.onDecision         || null;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    this.enabled = !!this.endpoint;\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192    // Per-character countdown timers  { name \u2192 ms until next decision }\n    39\u2192    this._timers = new Map();\n    40\u2192    // Prevent overlapping requests  { name \u2192 true }\n    41\u2192    this._pending = new Set();\n    42\u2192  }\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Called by SimulationEngine each frame \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  update(deltaMs, characters, objects, simState) {\n    47\u2192    if (!this.enabled) return;\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    for (const char of characters) {\n    50\u2192      let t = this._timers.get(char.name) ?? 0;\n    51\u2192      t -= deltaMs;\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192      // Don't make decisions while character is held at an object or in conversation;\n    54\u2192      // let the timer keep ticking so the cooldown UI stays accurate.\n    55\u2192      if (t <= 0 && !this._pending.has(char.name) && char.action !== 'interacting' && char.action !== 'talking') {\n    56\u2192        this._timers.set(char.name, this.decisionIntervalMs);\n    57\u2192        this._requestDecision(char, characters, objects, simState);\n    58\u2192      } else {\n    59\u2192        this._timers.set(char.name, t);\n    60\u2192      }\n    61\u2192    }\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Internal \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192  async _requestDecision(character, characters, objects, simState) {\n    67\u2192    this._pending.add(character.name);\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    try {\n    70\u2192      const nearbyObjects = objects\n    71\u2192        .filter(obj => {\n    72\u2192          const dx = obj.tx - character.tx;\n    73\u2192          const dy = obj.ty - character.ty;\n    74\u2192          return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) <= 8;\n    75\u2192        })\n    76\u2192        .map(o => {\n    77\u2192          const slot = o.getSlotInfo();\n    78\u2192          // Resolve reserved/occupied character names \u2192 display labels for LLM context\n    79\u2192          const reservedBy = (o.getReservations?.() ?? [])\n    80\u2192            .map(n => characters.find(c => c.name === n)?.label ?? n);\n    81\u2192          const occupiedBy = (o.getOccupants?.() ?? [])\n    82\u2192            .map(n => characters.find(c => c.name === n)?.label ?? n);\n    83\u2192          return {\n    84\u2192            id:         o.id,\n    85\u2192            label:      o.label,\n    86\u2192            state:      o.state,\n    87\u2192            slots:      `${slot.available}/${slot.capacity} available`,\n    88\u2192            available:  slot.available > 0,\n    89\u2192            // Blackboard: show who is already heading here or using it\n    90\u2192            intendedBy: reservedBy,\n    91\u2192            usedBy:     occupiedBy,\n    92\u2192          };\n    93\u2192        });\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192      const nearbyCharacters = characters\n    96\u2192        .filter(c => {\n    97\u2192          if (c.name === character.name) return false;\n    98\u2192          const dx = c.tx - character.tx;\n    99\u2192          const dy = c.ty - character.ty;\n   100\u2192          return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) <= 8;\n   101\u2192        })\n   102\u2192        .map(c => ({ label: c.label, tx: Math.round(c.tx), ty: Math.round(c.ty), action: c.action }));\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192      const systemPrompt =\n   105\u2192        `You are ${character.label}, a character in a cosy Japanese home simulation. ` +\n   106\u2192        `Decide what to do next based on your mood (${character.mood}/100) and energy (${character.energy}/100). ` +\n   107\u2192        `Reply ONLY with valid JSON \u2014 no markdown fences, no extra text.`;\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192      const userPrompt =\n   110\u2192        `Sim time: ${simState.timeString}. ` +\n   111\u2192        `You are at tile (${Math.round(character.tx)}, ${Math.round(character.ty)}). ` +\n   112\u2192        `Current action: ${character.action}. ` +\n   113\u2192        `Nearby objects: ${JSON.stringify(nearbyObjects)}. ` +\n   114\u2192        `Nearby characters: ${JSON.stringify(nearbyCharacters)}. ` +\n   115\u2192        `Choose one action:\\n` +\n   116\u2192        `  { \"action\": \"move\",     \"targetTile\": {\"tx\": N, \"ty\": N}, \"dialogue\": \"...\", \"description\": \"...\" }\\n` +\n   117\u2192        `  { \"action\": \"interact\", \"targetId\": \"object_id\",          \"dialogue\": \"...\", \"description\": \"...\" }\\n` +\n   118\u2192        `  { \"action\": \"idle\",                                        \"dialogue\": \"...\", \"description\": \"...\" }\\n` +\n   119\u2192        `  { \"action\": \"talk\",     \"targetCharacter\": \"character_label\", \"dialogue\": \"what you say\", \"targetDialogue\": \"their response\", \"description\": \"...\" }`;\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192      const headers = { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' };\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192      // Build body \u2014 compatible with Anthropic Messages API shape by default;\n   124\u2192      // swap for OpenAI chat completions if needed by changing endpoint.\n   125\u2192      const body = JSON.stringify({\n   126\u2192        model: this.model,\n   127\u2192        max_tokens: 256,\n   128\u2192        system: systemPrompt,\n   129\u2192        messages: [{ role: 'user', content: userPrompt }],\n   130\u2192      });\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192      const res = await fetch(this.endpoint, { method: 'POST', headers, body });\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192      if (!res.ok) {\n   135\u2192        console.warn(`[AIEngine] ${character.name}: HTTP ${res.status}`);\n   136\u2192        return;\n   137\u2192      }\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192      const data = await res.json();\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192      // Extract text from Anthropic-style response; fall back to OpenAI style\n   142\u2192      let text = data?.content?.[0]?.text     // Anthropic\n   143\u2192              ?? data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content // OpenAI\n   144\u2192              ?? null;\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192      if (!text) {\n   147\u2192        console.warn(`[AIEngine] ${character.name}: unrecognised response shape`, data);\n   148\u2192        return;\n   149\u2192      }\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192      const decision = JSON.parse(text);\n   152\u2192      this._applyDecision(character, decision, characters, objects, simState);\n   153\u2192    } catch (err) {\n   154\u2192      console.warn(`[AIEngine] ${character.name}:`, err);\n   155\u2192    } finally {\n   156\u2192      this._pending.delete(character.name);\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  _applyDecision(character, decision, characters, objects, simState) {\n   161\u2192    if (!decision?.action) return;\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    switch (decision.action) {\n   164\u2192      case 'move':\n   165\u2192        if (decision.targetTile) {\n   166\u2192          // Release any pending object reservation before moving freely\n   167\u2192          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n   168\u2192            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n   169\u2192            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n   170\u2192            character.targetObjectId = null;\n   171\u2192          }\n   172\u2192          character.moveTo(decision.targetTile.tx, decision.targetTile.ty);\n   173\u2192        }\n   174\u2192        break;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192      case 'interact': {\n   177\u2192        const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId);\n   178\u2192        if (obj) {\n   179\u2192          // If already heading to a different object, release that reservation first\n   180\u2192          if (character.targetObjectId && character.targetObjectId !== obj.id) {\n   181\u2192            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n   182\u2192            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n   183\u2192            character.targetObjectId = null;\n   184\u2192          }\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192          // Only commit if a slot is available and object is currently interactable\n   187\u2192          const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];\n   188\u2192          if (!obj.isFull() && stateDef?.transitions?.use) {\n   189\u2192            // Walk to an adjacent walkable tile (same pattern as wander engine)\n   190\u2192            const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(obj.tx, obj.ty);\n   191\u2192            if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {\n   192\u2192              if (obj.reserve(character.name)) { // claim slot; guard against any race\n   193\u2192                character.targetObjectId = obj.id;\n   194\u2192                character.pendingAction  = 'use';\n   195\u2192              }\n   196\u2192            }\n   197\u2192          }\n   198\u2192        }\n   199\u2192        break;\n   200\u2192      }\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192      case 'idle':\n   203\u2192        // Release any pending reservation, then stay put for one full decision interval\n   204\u2192        if (character.targetObjectId) {\n   205\u2192          const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n   206\u2192          if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n   207\u2192          character.targetObjectId = null;\n   208\u2192        }\n   209\u2192        character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;\n   210\u2192        break;\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192      case 'talk': {\n   213\u2192        const target = characters.find(c => c.label === decision.targetCharacter);\n   214\u2192        if (target) {\n   215\u2192          // Evaluate Phase: check if B can accept a conversation request.\n   216\u2192          // Accept if idle or walking; reject if interacting, talking, or otherwise busy.\n   217\u2192          const canAccept = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192          if (!canAccept) {\n   220\u2192            // B is busy \u2014 log the rejection and let A fall back to idle/wander\n   221\u2192            if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n   222\u2192              simState.pushEvent({\n   223\u2192                type:           'talk_rejected',\n   224\u2192                initiatorLabel: character.label,\n   225\u2192                targetLabel:    target.label,\n   226\u2192                reason:         target.action,\n   227\u2192              });\n   228\u2192            }\n   229\u2192            // A idles for the normal decision interval (fallback handled by Engine wander)\n   230\u2192            character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;\n   231\u2192            break;\n   232\u2192          }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192          // Release any previous object reservation before walking to talk\n   235\u2192          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n   236\u2192            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n   237\u2192            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n   238\u2192            character.targetObjectId = null;\n   239\u2192          }\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192          // Request Phase: walk A to a tile adjacent to B.\n   242\u2192          // Arrival handler in Engine will start the shared talking state.\n   243\u2192          const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty));\n   244\u2192          if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {\n   245\u2192            // Store pending talk so Engine knows what to do on arrival\n   246\u2192            character.pendingTalkTarget   = target;\n   247\u2192            character.pendingTalkDialogue = decision.dialogue       || null;\n   248\u2192            character.pendingTalkResponse = decision.targetDialogue || null;\n   249\u2192          }\n   250\u2192        }\n   251\u2192        break;\n   252\u2192      }\n   253\u2192    }\n   254\u2192\n console.log(`[${character.label}] ${decision.description}`);\n   263\u2192    }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192    if (this.onDecision) {\n   266\u2192      this.onDecision(character, decision, objects, }
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #34
Now let me look at how `debug.html` sets up `simEngine` and if it uses `index.html` code or has its own setup.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   400→      if (ACTIVE_STATES.has(state)) return 'active';
   401→      return 'other';
   402→    }
   403→
   404→    // ── Log helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   405→
   406→    function pushLog(objectId, fromState, toState, trigger) {
   407→      log.unshift({ objectId, fromState, toState, trigger, time: simEngine.getSimTimeString() });
   408→      if (log.length > MAX_LOG) log.length = MAX_LOG;
   409→      logDirty = true;
   410→    }
   411→
   412→    function pushAILog(charLabel, action, target, dialogue) {
   413→      log.unshift({ type: 'ai', charLabel, action, target, dialogue, time: simEngine.getSimTimeString() });
   414→      if (log.length > MAX_LOG) log.length = MAX_LOG;
   415→      logDirty = true;
   416→    }
   417→
   418→    // ── Manual interact (called from interaction buttons) ─────────────────────
   419→
   420→    window._dbInteract = function(objectId, action) {
   421→      const obj = simEngine.objectEngine.getById(objectId);
   422→      if (!obj) return;
   423→      const from = obj.state;
   424→      const ev = simEngine.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);
   425→      if (ev) {
   426→        pushLog(objectId, from, ev.state, action);
   427→        prevStateMap.set(objectId, ev.state);
   428→        updateObjectCard(obj);
   429→      }
   430→    };
   431→
   432→    // ── Build DOM cards (once) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   433→
   434→    function initObjectCards() {
   435→      const grid = document.getElementById('objects-grid');
   436→      for (const obj of simEngine.objectEngine.getAll()) {
   437→        const card = document.createElement('div');
   438→        card.className = 'object-card';
   439→        card.id = `obj-${obj.id}`;
   440→        card.innerHTML = `
   441→          <div class="object-card-header">
   442→            <span class="object-name">${obj.name}</span>
   443→            <span class="state-badge" id="badge-${obj.id}"></span>
   444→          </div>
   445→          <div class="position-hint">tile (${obj.tx}, ${obj.ty})</div>
   446→          <div class="slot-info" id="slots-${obj.id}"></div>
   447→          <div class="timer-bar" id="timerbar-${obj.id}" style="display:none">
   448→            <div class="timer-fill" id="timerfill-${obj.id}" style="width:0%"></div>
   449→          </div>
   450→          <div class="timer-text" id="timertext-${obj.id}" style="display:none"></div>
   451→          <div class="interactions" id="int-${obj.id}"></div>
   452→        `;
   453→        grid.appendChild(card);
   454→        updateObjectCard(obj); // initial paint
   455→      }
   456→    }
   457→
   458→    function updateObjectCard(obj) {
   459→      const badge      = document.getElementById(`badge-${obj.id}`);
   460→      const timerBar   = document.getElementById(`timerbar-${obj.id}`);
   461→      const timerFill  = document.getElementById(`timerfill-${obj.id}`);
   462→      const timerText  = document.getElementById(`timertext-${obj.id}`);
   463→      const intDiv     = document.getElementById(`int-${obj.id}`);
   464→      if (!badge) return;
   465→
   466→      const prevState = badge.dataset.state;
   467→      const stateChanged = prevState !== obj.state;
   468→
   469→      if (stateChanged) {
   470→        badge.textContent = obj.state;
   471→        badge.className = `state-badge ${badgeClass(obj.state)}`;
   472→        badge.dataset.state = obj.state;
   473→
   474→        // Rebuild interaction buttons on state change
   475→        const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   476→        const actions = Object.keys(stateDef?.transitions || {});
   477→        intDiv.innerHTML = actions.map(a =>
   478→          `<button onclick="window._dbInteract('${obj.id}','${a}')">${a}</button>`
   479→        ).join('');
   480→      }
   481→
   482→      // Slot occupancy (update every frame)
   483→      const slotDiv = document.getElementById(`slots-${obj.id}`);
   484→      if (slotDiv) {
   485→        const si          = obj.getSlotInfo();
   486→        const reservations = obj.getReservations ? obj.getReservations() : [];
   487→        const occupants    = obj.getOccupants    ? obj.getOccupants()    : [];
   488→
   489→        // Resolve internal char names → display labels via simEngine.characters
   490→        const labelFor = name => simEngine.characters.find(c => c.name === name)?.label ?? name;
   491→
   492→        const pips = [];
   493→        for (let i = 0; i < si.capacity; i++) {
   494→          let cls   = 'available';
   495→          let title = 'available';
   496→          if (i < occupants.length) {
   497→            cls   = 'occupied';
   498→            title = `using: ${labelFor(occupants[i])}`;
   499→          } else if (i < occupants.length + reservations.length) {
   500→            cls   = 'reserved';
   501→            title = `heading here: ${labelFor(reservations[i - occupants.length])}`;
   502→          }
   503→          pips.push(`<div class="slot-pip ${cls}" title="${title}"></div>`);
   504→        }
   505→        const labelCls = si.available === 0 ? 'slot-label full' : 'slot-label';
   506→        const labelTxt = si.available === 0 ? 'full' : `${si.available}/${si.capacity} free`;
   507→        slotDiv.innerHTML = `<div class="slot-pips">${pips.join('')}</div><span class="${labelCls}">${labelTxt}</span>`;
   508→      }
   509→
   510→      // Timer bar (update every frame regardless of state change)
   511→      const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   512→      const hasTimer = !!(stateDef?.auto_next && obj.autoTimer > 0);
   513→      if (hasTimer) {
   514→        const totalMs = stateDef.auto_next.after * 1000;
   515→        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - obj.autoTimer / totalMs) * 100));
   516→        timerBar.style.display = '';
   517→        timerFill.style.width  = `${pct.toFixed(1)}%`;
   518→        timerText.style.display = '';
   519→        timerText.textContent   = `${Math.ceil(obj.autoTimer / 1000)}s remaining`;

</system-reminder>

SUCCESS 
   520→      } else {
   521→        timerBar.style.display  = 'none';
   522→        timerText.style.display = 'none';
   523→      }
   524→    }
   525→
   526→    function initCharacterCards() {
   527→      const grid = document.getElementById('characters-grid');
   528→      simEngine.characters.forEach((char, i) => {
   529→        const card = document.createElement('div');
   530→        card.className = 'character-card';
   531→        card.id = `char-${i}`;
   532→        grid.appendChild(card);
   533→        updateCharacterCard(char, i); // initial paint
   534→      });
   535→    }
   536→
   537→    function updateCharacterCard(char, i) {
   538→      const card = document.getElementById(`char-${i}`);
   539→      if (!card) return;
   540→
   541→      // Build a descriptive intent label: what the character is doing / heading to
   542→      const targetName = char.talkTarget
   543→        ? `\u2194 ${char.talkTarget.label}`          // ↔ talking
   544→        : char.pendingTalkTarget
   545→          ? `\u2192 ${char.pendingTalkTarget.label} (following)` // → following
   546→          : char.interactTarget
   547→            ? `\u25c6 ${simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.interactTarget)?.name ?? char.interactTarget} (using)` // ◆ using
   548→            : char.targetObjectId
   549→              ? `\u2192 ${simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.targetObjectId)?.name ?? char.targetObjectId} (reserved)` // → reserved
   550→              : '\u2014'; // —
   551→
   552→      // Hold countdown timer (shown while character is locked at an object or talking)
   553→      let holdHtml = '';
   554→      if (char.action === 'interacting' && char.interactTimer > 0 && char.interactTimerMax > 0) {
   555→        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - char.interactTimer / char.interactTimerMax) * 100));
   556→        holdHtml = `
   557→          <div class="char-row" style="margin-top:4px">
   558→            <span>Hold</span>
   559→            <span class="char-value">${Math.ceil(char.interactTimer / 1000)}s</span>
   560→          </div>
   561→          <div class="stat-bar">
   562→            <div class="stat-fill hold-timer" style="width:${pct.toFixed(1)}%"></div>
   563→          </div>
   564→        `;
   565→      } else if (char.action === 'talking' && char.talkTimer > 0 && char.talkTimerMax > 0) {
   566→        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - char.talkTimer / char.talkTimerMax) * 100));
   567→        holdHtml = `
   568→          <div class="char-row" style="margin-top:4px">
   569→            <span>Talk</span>
   570→            <span class="char-value">${Math.ceil(char.talkTimer / 1000)}s</span>
   571→          </div>
   572→          <div class="stat-bar">
   573→            <div class="stat-fill hold-timer" style="width:${pct.toFixed(1)}%"></div>
   574→          </div>
   575→        `;
   576→      }
   577→
   578→      // AI decision cooldown timer
   579→      let cooldownHtml = '';
   580→      if (aiEngine.enabled) {
   581→        const remaining  = Math.max(0, aiEngine._timers.get(char.name) ?? aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs);
   582→        const pct        = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - remaining / aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs) * 100));
   583→        const secRemaining = Math.ceil(remaining / 1000);
   584→        cooldownHtml = `
   585→          <div class="char-row" style="margin-top:4px">
   586→            <span>AI next</span>
   587→            <span class="char-value">${secRemaining}s</span>
   588→          </div>
   589→          <div class="stat-bar">
   590→            <div class="stat-fill ai-cooldown" style="width:${pct.toFixed(1)}%"></div>
   591→          </div>
   592→        `;
   593→      }
   594→
   595→      card.innerHTML = `
   596→        <div class="character-name">${char.label}</div>
   597→        <div class="char-row">
   598→          <span>Action</span>
   599→          <span class="action-badge ${char.action}">${char.action}</span>
   600→        </div>
   601→        <div class="char-row">
   602→          <span>Position</span>
   603→          <span class="char-value">(${Math.round(char.tx)}, ${Math.round(char.ty)})</span>
   604→        </div>
   605→        <div class="char-row">
   606→          <span>Facing</span>
   607→          <span class="char-value">${char.facing}</span>
   608→        </div>
   609→        <div class="char-row">
   610→          <span>Target</span>
   611→          <span class="char-value">${targetName}</span>
   612→        </div>
   613→        <div class="char-row" style="margin-top:4px">
   614→          <span>Mood</span>
   615→          <span class="char-value">${char.mood}</span>
   616→        </div>
   617→        <div class="stat-bar">
   618→          <div class="stat-fill mood" style="width:${char.mood}%"></div>
   619→        </div>
   620→        <div class="char-row" style="margin-top:4px">
   621→          <span>Energy</span>
   622→          <span class="char-value">${char.energy}</span>
   623→        </div>
   624→        <div class="stat-bar">
   625→          <div class="stat-fill energy" style="width:${char.energy}%"></div>
   626→        </div>
   627→        ${holdHtml}
   628→        ${cooldownHtml}
   629→      `;
   630→    }
   631→
   632→    function renderEventLog() {
   633→      if (!logDirty) return;
   634→      logDirty = false;
   635→
   636→      const el = document.getElementById('event-log');
   637→      if (log.length === 0) {
   638→        el.innerHTML = '<div style="color:#555;padding:4px 0">No events yet.</div>';
   639→        return;
   640→      }
   641→      el.innerHTML = log.slice(0, 80).map(e => {
   642→        if (e.type === 'ai') {
   643→          const targetStr   = e.target   ? ` \u2192 ${e.target}` : '';
   644→          const dialogueStr = e.dialogue ? ` \u201c${e.dialogue}\u201d` : '';
   645→          return `
   646→            <div class="log-entry ai">
   647→              <span class="log-time">${e.time}</span>
   648→              <span class="log-obj">${e.charLabel}</span>
   649→              <span class="log-transition">${e.action}${targetStr}${dialogueStr}</span>
   650→              <span class="log-tag ai">AI</span>
   651→            </div>
   652→          `;
   653→        }
   654→        if (e.type === 'conversation_start') {
   655→          return `
   656→            <div class="log-entry talk">
   657→              <span class="log-time">${e.time}</span>
   658→              <span class="log-obj">${e.initiatorLabel}</span>
