525\u2192\n   526\u2192    function initCharacterCards() {\n   527\u2192      const grid = document.getElementById('characters-grid');\n   528\u2192      simEngine.characters.forEach((char, i) => {\n   529\u2192        const card = document.createElement('div');\n   530\u2192        card.className = 'character-card';\n   531\u2192        card.id = `char-${i}`;\n   532\u2192        grid.appendChild(card);\n   533\u2192        updateCharacterCard(char, i); // initial paint\n   534\u2192      });\n   535\u2192    }\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192    function updateCharacterCard(char, i) {\n   538\u2192      const card = document.getElementById(`char-${i}`);\n   539\u2192      if (!card) return;\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192      // Build a descriptive intent label: what the character is doing / heading to\n   542\u2192      const targetName = char.talkTarget\n   543\u2192        ? `\\u2194 ${char.talkTarget.label}`          // \u2194 talking\n   544\u2192        : char.pendingTalkTarget\n   545\u2192          ? `\\u2192 ${char.pendingTalkTarget.label} (following)` // \u2192 following\n   546\u2192          : char.interactTarget\n   547\u2192            ? `\\u25c6 ${simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.interactTarget)?.name ?? char.interactTarget} (using)` // \u25c6 using\n   548\u2192            : char.targetObjectId\n   549\u2192              ? `\\u2192 ${simEngine.objectEngine.getById(char.targetObjectId)?.name ?? char.targetObjectId} (reserved)` // \u2192 reserved\n   550\u2192              : '\\u2014'; // \u2014\n   551\u2192\n   552\u2192      // Hold countdown timer (shown while character is locked at an object or talking)\n   553\u2192      let holdHtml = '';\n   554\u2192      if (char.action === 'interacting' && char.interactTimer > 0 && char.interactTimerMax > 0) {\n   555\u2192        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - char.interactTimer / char.interactTimerMax) * 100));\n   556\u2192        holdHtml = `\n   557\u2192          <div class=\"char-row\" style=\"margin-top:4px\">\n   558\u2192            <span>Hold</span>\n   559\u2192            <span class=\"char-value\">${Math.ceil(char.interactTimer / 1000)}s</span>\n   560\u2192          </div>\n   561\u2192          <div class=\"stat-bar\">\n   562\u2192            <div class=\"stat-fill hold-timer\" style=\"width:${pct.toFixed(1)}%\"></div>\n   563\u2192          </div>\n   564\u2192        `;\n   565\u2192      } else if (char.action === 'talking' && char.talkTimer > 0 && char.talkTimerMax > 0) {\n   566\u2192        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - char.talkTimer / char.talkTimerMax) * 100));\n   567\u2192        holdHtml = `\n   568\u2192          <div class=\"char-row\" style=\"margin-top:4px\">\n   569\u2192            <span>Talk</span>\n   570\u2192            <span class=\"char-value\">${Math.ceil(char.talkTimer / 1000)}s</span>\n   571\u2192          </div>\n   572\u2192          <div class=\"stat-bar\">\n   573\u2192            <div class=\"stat-fill hold-timer\" style=\"width:${pct.toFixed(1)}%\"></div>\n   574\u2192          </div>\n   575\u2192        `;\n   576\u2192      }\n   577\u2192\n   578\u2192      // AI decision cooldown timer\n   579\u2192      let cooldownHtml = '';\n   580\u2192      if (aiEngine.enabled) {\n   581\u2192        const remaining  = Math.max(0, aiEngine._timers.get(char.name) ?? aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs);\n   582\u2192        const pct        = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - remaining / aiEngine.decisionIntervalMs) * 100));\n   583\u2192        const secRemaining = Math.ceil(remaining / 1000);\n   584\u2192        cooldownHtml = `\n   585\u2192          <div class=\"char-row\" style=\"margin-top:4px\">\n   586\u2192            <span>AI next</span>\n   587\u2192            <span class=\"char-value\">${secRemaining}s</span>\n   588\u2192          </div>\n   589\u2192          <div class=\"stat-bar\">\n   590\u2192            <div class=\"stat-fill ai-cooldown\" style=\"width:${pct.toFixed(1)}%\"></div>\n   591\u2192          </div>\n   592\u2192        `;\n   593\u2192      }\n   594\u2192\n   595\u2192      card.innerHTML = `\n   596\u2192        <div class=\"character-name\">${char.label}</div>\n   597\u2192        <div class=\"char-row\">\n   598\u2192          <span>Action</span>\n   599\u2192          <span class=\"action-badge ${char.action}\">${char.action}</span>\n   600\u2192        </div>\n   601\u2192        <div class=\"char-row\">\n   602\u2192          <span>Position</span>\n   603\u2192          <span class=\"char-value\">(${Math.round(char.tx)}, ${Math.round(char.ty)})</span>\n   604\u2192        </div>\n   605\u2192        <div class=\"char-row\">\n   606\u2192          <span>Facing</span>\n   607\u2192          <span class=\"char-value\">${char.facing}</span>\n   608\u2192        </div>\n   609\u2192        <div class=\"char-row\">\n   610\u2192          <span>Target</span>\n   611\u2192          <span class=\"char-value\">${targetName}</span>\n   612\u2192        </div>\n   613\u2192        <div class=\"char-row\" style=\"margin-top:4px\">\n   614\u2192          <span>Mood</span>\n   615\u2192          <span class=\"char-value\">${char.mood}</span>\n   616\u2192        </div>\n   617\u2192        <div class=\"stat-bar\">\n   618\u2192          <div class=\"stat-fill mood\" style=\"width:${char.mood}%\"></div>\n   619\u2192        </div>\n   620\u2192        <div class=\"char-row\" style=\"margin-top:4px\">\n   621\u2192          <span>Energy</span>\n   622\u2192          <span class=\"char-value\">${char.energy}</span>\n   623\u2192        </div>\n   624\u2192        <div class=\"stat-bar\">\n   625\u2192          <div class=\"stat-fill energy\" style=\"width:${char.energy}%\"></div>\n   626\u2192        </div>\n   627\u2192        ${holdHtml}\n   628\u2192        ${cooldownHtml}\n   629\u2192      `;\n   630\u2192    }\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192    function renderEventLog() {\n   633\u2192      if (!logDirty) return;\n   634\u2192      logDirty = false;\n   635\u2192\n   636\u2192      const el = document.getElementById('event-log');\n   637\u2192      if (log.length === 0) {\n   638\u2192        el.innerHTML = '<div style=\"color:#555;padding:4px 0\">No events yet.</div>';\n   639\u2192        return;\n   640\u2192      }\n   641\u2192      el.innerHTML = log.slice(0, 80).map(e => {\n   642\u2192        if (e.type === 'ai') {\n   643\u2192          const targetStr   = e.target   ? ` \\u2192 ${e.target}` : '';\n   644\u2192          const dialogueStr = e.dialogue ? ` \\u201c${e.dialogue}\\u201d` : '';\n   645\u2192          return `\n   646\u2192            <div class=\"log-entry ai\">\n   647\u2192              <span class=\"log-time\">${e.time}</span>\n   648\u2192              <span class=\"log-obj\">${e.charLabel}</span>\n   649\u2192              <span class=\"log-transition\">${e.action}${targetStr}${dialogueStr}</span>\n   650\u2192              <span class=\"log-tag ai\">AI</span>\n   651\u2192            </div>\n   652\u2192          `;\n   653\u2192        }\n   654\u2192        if (e.type === 'conversation_start') {\n   655\u2192          return `\n   656\u2192            <div class=\"log-entry talk\">\n   657\u2192              <span class=\"log-time\">${e.time}</span>\n   658\u2192              <span class=\"log-obj\">${e.initiatorLabel}</span>\n   659\u2192              <span class=\"log-transition\">talking \\u2194    350→
   351→    // ── Controls ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   352→
   353→    const pauseBtn = document.getElementById('btn-pause');
   354→    pauseBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
   355→      paused = !paused;
   356→      pauseBtn.innerHTML = paused ? '&#9654; Play' : '&#9646;&#9646; Pause';
   357→      pauseBtn.classList.toggle('active', paused);
   358→      if (!paused) lastTs = null; // avoid time-jump on resume
   359→    });
   360→
   361→    document.querySelectorAll('.speed-btn').forEach(btn => {
   362→      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
   363→        speed = parseFloat(btn.dataset.speed);
   364→        document.querySelectorAll('.speed-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
   365→        btn.classList.add('active');
   366→      });
   367→    });
   368→
   369→    document.getElementById('btn-reset').addEventListener('click', () => {
   370→      // Reset object states and slot tracking to defaults
   371→      for (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {
   372→        const obj = simEngine.objectEngine.getById(def.id);
   373→        if (!obj) continue;
   374→        obj.state = def.state || 'idle';
   375→        obj._resetAutoTimer();
   376→        obj.clearSlots();
   377→        prevStateMap.set(def.id, obj.state);
   378→      }
   379→      // Reset sim clock
   380→      simEngine.simTimeMs = 8 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
   381→      // Clear log
   382→      log.length = 0;
   383→      logDirty = true;
   384→      // Update badge classes immediately
   385→      for (const obj of simEngine.objectEngine.getAll()) {
   386→        updateObjectCard(obj);
   387→      }
   388→    });
   389→
   390→    // ── Helper: state badge class ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   391→
   392→    const ACTIVE_STATES = new Set([
   393→      'cooking', 'brewing', 'painting', 'working', 'tending', 'reading', 'on',
   394→    ]);
   395→    const DONE_STATES = new Set(['done', 'ready', 'open', 'cozy', 'resting']);
   396→
   397→    function badgeClass(state) {
   398→      if (state === 'idle')         return 'idle';
   399→      if (DONE_STATES.has(state))   return 'done';
   400→      if (ACTIVE_STATES.has(state)) return 'active';
   401→      return 'other';
   402→    }
   403→
   404→    // ── Log helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   250→        <div>
   251→          <div class="section-title">Objects (10)</div>
   252→          <div id="objects-grid"></div>
   253→        </div>
   254→        <div>
   255→          <div class="section-title">Characters</div>
   256→          <div id="characters-grid"></div>
   257→        </div>
   258→      </div>
   259→      <div id="log-section">
   260→        <div class="section-title">Event Log</div>
   261→        <div id="event-log"><div style="color:#555;padding:4px 0">No events yet.</div></div>
   262→      </div>
   263→    </div>
   264→  </div>
   265→
   266→  <script type="module">
   267→    import { ObjectEngine, OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';
   268→    import { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';
   269→    import { Character } from './src/Character.js';
   270→    import { AIEngine } from './src/AIEngine.js';
   271→
   272→    // ── Walkable grid (mirrors scene.js) ──────────────────────────────────────
   273→
   274→    const GRID_COLS = 19;
   275→    const GRID_ROWS = 13;
   276→
   277→    function buildWalkableGrid() {
   278→      const grid = [];
   279→      for (let row = 0; row < GRID_ROWS; row++) {
   280→        const r = [];
   281→        for (let col = 0; col < GRID_COLS; col++) {
   282→          r.push(col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9);
   283→        }
   284→        grid.push(r);
   285→      }
   286→      // Mark tiles occupied by objects as non-walkable
   287→      for (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {
   288→        const { tx, ty } = def.position;
   289→        if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) {
   290→          grid[ty][tx] = false;
   291→        }
   292→      }
   293→      return grid;
   294→    }
   295→
   296→    // ── Character definitions (mirrors scene.js) ─────────────────────────────
   297→
   298→    const CHARS_CONFIG = [
   299→      { name: 'Amelia', label: 'Maple', tx: 6,  ty: 7,
   300→        dialogue: ['Perfect brew today~', 'Want some tea?'] },
   301→      { name: 'Lucy',   label: 'Sol',   tx: 10, ty: 6,
   302→        dialogue: ['The bonsai looks healthy', 'Such peaceful light...'] },
   303→      { name: 'Ash',    label: 'Nyx',   tx: 13, ty: 5,
   304→        dialogue: ['I need more paint...', 'This light is perfect'] },
   305→    ];
   306→
   307→    // ── Engine setup ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   308→
   309→    const walkable = buildWalkableGrid();
   310→
   311→    const aiEngine = new AIEngine({
   312→      endpoint: '/api/llm',
   313→      model: 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku',
   314→      decisionIntervalMs: 30_000,
   315→      onDecision: (char, decision, objects) => {
   316→        const targetName = decision.targetId
   317→          ? (objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId)?.name ?? decision.targetId)
   318→          : decision.targetTile
   319→            ? `(${decision.targetTile.tx},${decision.targetTile.ty})`
   320→            : decision.targetCharacter
   321→              ?? null;
   322→        pushAILog(char.label, decision.action, targetName, decision.dialogue);
   323→      },
   324→    });
   325→
   326→    const simEngine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS, aiEngine });
   327→
   328→    for (const cfg of CHARS_CONFIG) {
   329→      simEngine.addCharacter(new Character({
   330→        ...cfg, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS,
   331→      }));
   332→    }
   333→
   334→    // ── UI state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   335→
   336→    let paused = false;
   337→    let speed  = 1;
   338→    let lastTs = null;
   339→
   340→    // Event log (newest-first)
   341→    const log = [];
   342→    const MAX_LOG = 150;
   343→    let logDirty = false;
   344→
   345→    // Track previous object states so we can log from→to
   346→    const prevStateMap = new Map();
   347→    for (const obj of simEngine.objectEngine.getAll()) {
   348→      prevStateMap.set(obj.id, obj.state);
   349→    }

    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "   250\u2192        <div>\n   251\u2192          <div class=\"section-title\">Objects (10)</div>\n   252\u2192          <div id=\"objects-grid\"></div>\n   253\u2192        </div>\n   254\u2192        <div>\n   255\u2192          <div class=\"section-title\">Characters</div>\n   256\u2192          <div id=\"characters-grid\"></div>\n   257\u2192        </div>\n   258\u2192      </div>\n   259\u2192      <div id=\"log-section\">\n   260\u2192        <div class=\"section-title\">Event Log</div>\n   261\u2192        <div id=\"event-log\"><div style=\"color:#555;padding:4px 0\">No events yet.</div></div>\n   262\u2192      </div>\n   263\u2192    </div>\n   264\u2192  </div>\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192  <script type=\"module\">\n   267\u2192    import { ObjectEngine, OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';\n   268\u2192    import { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';\n   269\u2192    import { Character } from './src/Character.js';\n   270\u2192    import { AIEngine } from './src/AIEngine.js';\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 Walkable grid (mirrors scene.js) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192    const GRID_COLS = 19;\n   275\u2192    const GRID_ROWS = 13;\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192    function buildWalkableGrid() {\n   278\u2192      const grid = [];\n   279\u2192      for (let row = 0; row < GRID_ROWS; row++) {\n   280\u2192        const r = [];\n   281\u2192        for (let col = 0; col < GRID_COLS; col++) {\n   282\u2192          r.push(col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9);\n   283\u2192        }\n   284\u2192        grid.push(r);\n   285\u2192      }\n   286\u2192      // Mark tiles occupied by objects as non-walkable\n   287\u2192      for (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n   288\u2192        const { tx, ty } = def.position;\n   289\u2192        if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) {\n   290\u2192          grid[ty][tx] = false;\n   291\u2192        }\n   292\u2192      }\n   293\u2192      return grid;\n   294\u2192    }\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 Character definitions (mirrors scene.js) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192    const CHARS_CONFIG = [\n   299\u2192      { name: 'Amelia', label: 'Maple', tx: 6,  ty: 7,\n   300\u2192        dialogue: ['Perfect brew today~', 'Want some tea?'] },\n   301\u2192      { name: 'Lucy',   label: 'Sol',   tx: 10, ty: 6,\n   302\u2192        dialogue: ['The bonsai looks healthy', 'Such peaceful light...'] },\n   303\u2192      { name: 'Ash',    label: 'Nyx',   tx: 13, ty: 5,\n   304\u2192        dialogue: ['I need more paint...', 'This light is perfect'] },\n   305\u2192    ];\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 Engine setup \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192    const walkable = buildWalkableGrid();\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192    const aiEngine = new AIEngine({\n   312\u2192      endpoint: '/api/llm',\n   313\u2192      model: 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku',\n   314\u2192      decisionIntervalMs: 30_000,\n   315\u2192      onDecision: (char, decision, objects) => {\n   316\u2192        const targetName = decision.targetId\n   317\u2192          ? (objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId)?.name ?? decision.targetId)\n   318\u2192          : decision.targetTile\n   319\u2192            ? `(${decision.targetTile.tx},${decision.targetTile.ty})`\n   320\u2192            : decision.targetCharacter\n   321\u2192              ?? null;\n   322\u2192        pushAILog(char.label, decision.action, targetName, decision.dialogue);\n   323\u2192      },\n   324\u2192    });\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192    const simEngine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS, aiEngine });\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192    for (const cfg of CHARS_CONFIG) {\n   329\u2192      simEngine.addCharacter(new Character({\n   330\u2192        ...cfg, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS,\n   331\u2192      }));\n   332\u2192    }\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 UI state \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    let paused = false;\n   337\u2192    let speed  = 1;\n   338\u2192    let lastTs = null;\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192    // Event log (newest-first)\n   341\u2192    const log = [];\n   342\u2192    const MAX_LOG = 150;\n   343\u2192    let logDirty = false;\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192    // Track previous object states so we can log from\u2192to\n   346\u2192    const prevStateMap = new Map();\n   347\u2192    for (const obj of simEngine.objectEngine.getAll()) {\n   348\u2192      prevStateMap.set(obj.id, obj.state);\n   349\u2192    }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #44
Now I have a clear picture of the setup. Let me re-read the fallback event flow: 1. Engine.js `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` pushes to `this.pendingEvents` with type `'fallback'` 2. debug.html tick loop reads from `simEngine.pendingEvents.shift()` and handles type `'fallback'` The code looks correct, but let me check whether the conditions under which `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` is called are actually being triggered. Looking at: ```javascript _handleObjectArrival(char) { const objectId = char.targetObjectId; ``` I see the method clears the targeting immediately, attempts an interaction, and if that fails it releases the object and triggers the fallback. Now I need to trace back to see what's actually calling `_handleObjectArrival` and whether those arrival conditions are being met in the first place. The fallback event isn't showing up in the logs even though characters are visibly redirecting, which suggests the event is being created but never processed. I'm looking at the tick loop to trace where events get consumed—specifically checking if the pending events queue is being drained properly or if something's preventing the fallback event from being logged before it's consumed. I'm checking the investigation hints more carefully now. The function `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` is pushing a fallback event with specific fields like `fromLabel` and `toLabel`, and I need to verify that the render code in debug.html is reading these fields correctly and matching the event type string exactly. I'm tracing through the `_doWander` function to understand how characters select targets—it filters out full objects and only reserves slots for available ones, so characters should never attempt to use a full object in the first place. When a character arrives at their reserved object, `_handleObjectArrival` converts that reservation into active occupancy, but if `interact()` returns null (meaning no valid state transition exists), the character falls back to finding an alternative object instead. The redirect behavior is definitely happening when targets are full, but I'm wondering if the fallback event is getting lost somewhere in the process. Let me trace through when `_fallbackToAlternative` is actually being called and whether the event is properly being emitted.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

     1→/**
     2→ * objects.js — interactive object state machines for the Japanese Home scene.
     3→ *
     4→ * Each object definition has:
     5→ *   { id, name, position: { tx, ty }, interactMs, state, states }
     6→ *
     7→ * Each state can have:
     8→ *   transitions: { use: { next: 'stateName' } }   — triggered by interact()
     9→ *   auto_next:   { state: 'stateName', after: N }  — fires automatically after N seconds
    10→ *   speech:      'string'                           — shown as character speech bubble
    11→ *   emoji:       '🍳'                               — floating emoji on transition
    12→ */
    13→

   167→        emoji: '💤',
   168→        speech: 'Just a little nap...',
   169→        auto_next: { state: 'idle', after: 60 },
   170→        transitions: { use: { next: 'idle' } },
   171→      },
   172→    },
   173→  },
   174→  {
   175→    id: 'easel',
   176→    name: 'easel',
   177→    capacity: 1,
   178→    position: { tx: 2, ty: 7 },
   179→    interactMs: 12000,
   180→    state: 'idle',
   181→    states: {
   182→      idle: {
   183→        transitions: { use: { next: 'painting' } },
   184→      },
   185→      painting: {
   186→        speech: 'This light is perfect for painting~',
   187→        auto_next: { state: 'done', after: 45 },
   188→      },
   189→      done: {
   190→        emoji: '🎨',
   191→        speech: 'My masterpiece is complete!',
   192→        auto_next: { state: 'idle', after: 30 },
   193→        transitions: { use: { next: 'idle' } },
   194→      },
   195→    },
   196→  },
   197→  {
   198→    id: 'bonsai',
   199→    name: 'bonsai',
   200→    capacity: 1,
   201→    position: { tx: 11, ty: 8 },
   202→    interactMs: 5000,
   203→    state: 'idle',
   204→    states: {
   205→      idle: {
   206→        transitions: { use: { next: 'tending' } },
   207→      },
   208→      tending: {
   209→        speech: 'The bonsai looks healthy today.',
   210→        auto_next: { state: 'idle', after: 12 },
   211→      },
   212→    },
   213→  },
   214→];
   215→
   216→// ── ObjectInstance ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   217→
   218→class ObjectInstance {
   219→  constructor(def) {
   220→    this.id        = def.id;
   221→    this.name      = def.name;
   222→    this.label     = def.name;           // compat for AIEngine
   223→    this.position  = def.position;
   224→    this.tx        = def.position.tx;    // compat for AIEngine distance checks
   225→    this.ty        = def.position.ty;
   226→    this.states    = def.states;
   227→    this.state     = def.state || 'idle';
   228→    this.interactMs = def.interactMs || 8000;
   229→    this.autoTimer = 0;
   230→    this._resetAutoTimer();
   231→
   232→    // Slot system
   233→    this.capacity      = def.capacity ?? 1;
   234→    this._reservations = new Set(); // charNames walking toward this object
   235→    this._occupants    = new Set(); // charNames actively interacting
   236→  }
   237→
   238→  /** True if no more slots are available (reserved + occupied >= capacity). */
   239→  isFull() {
   240→    return this._reservations.size + this._occupants.size >= this.capacity;
   241→  }
   242→
   243→  /**
   244→   * Reserve a slot for charName before they start walking.
   245→   * Returns true if the reservation succeeded, false if the object is full.
   246→   */
   247→  reserve(charName) {
   248→    if (this.isFull()) return false;
   249→    this._reservations.add(charName);
   250→    return true;
   251→  }
   252→
   253→  /**
   254→   * Called when charName arrives at the object.
   255→   * Converts the reservation to an active occupancy.
   256→   */
   257→  arrive(charName) {
   258→    this._reservations.delete(charName);
   259→    this._occupants.add(charName);
   260→  }
   261→
   262→  /**
   263→   * Release a slot when the character finishes interacting or abandons the walk.
   264→   */
   265→  release(charName) {
   266→    this._reservations.delete(charName);
   267→    this._occupants.delete(charName);
   268→  }
   269→
   270→  /** Returns the names of characters who have reserved a slot (walking toward this object). */
   271→  getReservations() { return Array.from(this._reservations); }
   272→
   273→  /** Returns the names of characters who are actively occupying a slot. */
   274→  getOccupants() { return Array.from(this._occupants); }
   275→
   276→  /** Clear all reservations and occupancies (used on sim reset). */
   277→  clearSlots() {
   278→    this._reservations.clear();
   279→    this._occupants.clear();
   280→  }
   281→
   282→  /**
   283→   * Returns current slot occupancy info.
   284→   * @returns {{ capacity, reserved, occupied, available }}
   285→   */
   286→  getSlotInfo() {
   287→    return {
   288→      capacity:  this.capacity,
   289→      reserved:  this._reservations.size,
   290→      occupied:  this._occupants.size,
   291→      available: this.capacity - this._reservations.size - this._occupants.size,
   292→    };
   293→  }
   294→
   295→  _resetAutoTimer() {
   296→    const s = this.states[this.state];
   297→    this.autoTimer = s?.auto_next ? s.auto_next.after * 1000 : 0;
   298→  }
   299→
   300→  /**
   301→   * Trigger an interaction action (e.g. 'use').
   302→   * @returns {{ objectId, state, speech, emoji }|null}
   303→   */
   304→  interact(action = 'use') {
   305→    const s  = this.states[this.state];
   306→    const tr = s?.transitions?.[action];
   307→    if (!tr) return null;
   308→
   309→    this.state = tr.next;
   310→    this._resetAutoTimer();
   311→
   312→    const ns = this.states[this.state];
   313→    return {
   314→      objectId: this.id,
   315→      state:    this.state,
   316→      speech:   ns?.speech || null,
   317→      emoji:    ns?.emoji  || null,
   318→    };
   319→  }
   320→
   321→  /**
   322→   * Advance auto_next timers.
   323→   * @returns {{ objectId, state, emoji, auto: true }|null}
   324→   */
   325→  tick(deltaMs) {
   326→    if (this.autoTimer <= 0) return null;
   327→
   328→    this.autoTimer -= deltaMs;
   329→    if (this.autoTimer > 0) return null;
   330→
   331→    const s = this.states[this.state];
   332→    if (!s?.auto_next) return null;
   333→
   334→    this.state = s.auto_next.state;
   335→    this._resetAutoTimer();
   336→
   337→    const ns = this.states[this.state];
   338→    return {
   339→      objectId: this.id,
   340→      state:    this.state,
   341→      emoji:    ns?.emoji || null,
   342→      auto:     true,
   343→    };
   344→  }
   345→}
   346→
   347→// ── ObjectEngine ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   348→
   349→export class ObjectEngine {
   350→  constructor() {
   351→    this._map = new Map(); // id → ObjectInstance
   352→  }
   353→
   354→  addObjects(defs) {
   355→    for (const def of defs) {
   356→      this._map.set(def.id, new ObjectInstance(def));
   357→    }
   358→  }
   359→
   360→  /**
   361→   * Tick all object timers. Returns any events that fired this frame.
   362→   * @returns {Array<{ objectId, state, emoji, auto }>}
   363→   */
   364→  tick(deltaMs) {
   365→    const events = [];
   366→    for (const obj of this._map.values()) {
   367→      const ev = obj.tick(deltaMs);
   368→      if (ev) events.push(ev);
   369→    }
   370→    return events;
   371→  }
   372→
   373→  /**
   374→   * Trigger an action on an object (e.g. 'use').
   375→   * @returns {{ objectId, state, speech, emoji }|null}
   376→   */
   377→  interact(objectId, action = 'use') {
   378→    return this._map.get(objectId)?.interact(action) ?? null;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  getAll()    { return Array.from(this._map.values()); }
   382→  getById(id) { return this._map.get(id) ?? null; }
   383→
   384→  reserve(objectId, charName) { return this._map.get(objectId)?.reserve(charName) ?? false; }
   385→  arrive(objectId, charName)  { this._map.get(objectId)?.arrive(charName); }
   386→  release(objectId, charName) { this._map.get(objectId)?.release(charName); }
   387→  clearSlots(objectId)        { this._map.get(objectId)?.clearSlots(); }
   388→  clearAllSlots()             { for (const obj of this._map.values()) obj.clearSlots(); }
   389→}
   390→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

    "content": "     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192 * objects.js \u2014 interactive object state machines for the Japanese Home scene.\n     3\u2192 *\n     4\u2192 * Each object definition has:\n     5\u2192 *   { id, name, position: { tx, ty }, interactMs, state, states }\n     6\u2192 *\n     7\u2192 * Each state can have:\n     8\u2192 *   transitions: { use: { next: 'stateName' } }   \u2014 triggered by interact()\n     9\u2192 *   auto_next:   { state: 'stateName', after: N }  \u2014 fires automatically after N seconds\n    10\u2192 *   speech:      'string'                           \u2014 shown as character speech bubble\n    11\u2192 *   emoji:       '\ud83c\udf73'                               \u2014 floating emoji on transition\n    12\u2192 */\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Object definitions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192// \u2500\u2500 ObjectInstance \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192class ObjectInstance {\n   219\u2192  constructor(def) {\n   220\u2192    this.id        = def.id;\n   221\u2192    this.name      = def.name;\n   222\u2192    this.label     = def.name;           // compat for AIEngine\n   223\u2192    this.position  = def.position;\n   224\u2192    this.tx        = def.position.tx;    // compat for AIEngine distance checks\n   225\u2192    this.ty        = def.position.ty;\n   226\u2192    this.states    = def.states;\n   227\u2192    this.state     = def.state || 'idle';\n   228\u2192    this.interactMs = def.interactMs || 8000;\n   229\u2192    this.autoTimer = 0;\n   230\u2192    this._resetAutoTimer();\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    // Slot system\n   233\u2192    this.capacity      = def.capacity ?? 1;\n   234\u2192    this._reservations = new Set(); // charNames walking toward this object\n   235\u2192    this._occupants    = new Set(); // charNames actively interacting\n   236\u2192  }\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192  /** True if no more slots are available (reserved + occupied >= capacity). */\n   239\u2192  isFull() {\n   240\u2192    return this._reservations.size + this._occupants.size >= this.capacity;\n   241\u2192  }\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192  /**\n   244\u2192   * Reserve a slot for charName before they start walking.\n   245\u2192   * Returns true if the reservation succeeded, false if the object is full.\n   246\u2192   */\n   247\u2192  reserve(charName) {\n   248\u2192    if (this.isFull()) return false;\n   249\u2192    this._reservations.add(charName);\n   250\u2192    return true;\n   251\u2192  }\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192  /**\n   254\u2192   * Called when charName arrives at the object.\n   255\u2192   * Converts the reservation to an active occupancy.\n   256\u2192   */\n   257\u2192  arrive(charName) {\n   258\u2192    this._reservations.delete(charName);\n   259\u2192    this._occupants.add(charName);\n   260\u2192  }\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192  /**\n   263\u2192   * Release a slot when the character finishes interacting or abandons the walk.\n   264\u2192   */\n   265\u2192  release(charName) {\n   266\u2192    this._reservations.delete(charName);\n   267\u2192    this._occupants.delete(charName);\n   268\u2192  }\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192  /** Returns the names of characters who have reserved a slot (walking toward this object). */\n   271\u2192  getReservations() { return Array.from(this._reservations); }\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192  /** Returns the names of characters who are actively occupying a slot. */\n   274\u2192  getOccupants() { return Array.from(this._occupants); }\n   275\u2192\n   276\u2192  /** Clear all reservations and occupancies (used on sim reset). */\n   277\u2192  clearSlots() {\n   278\u2192    this._reservations.clear();\n   279\u2192    this._occupants.clear();\n   280\u2192  }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  /**\n   283\u2192   * Returns current slot occupancy info.\n   284\u2192   * @returns {{ capacity, reserved, occupied, available }}\n   285\u2192   */\n   286\u2192  getSlotInfo() {\n   287\u2192    return {\n   288\u2192      capacity:  this.capacity,\n   289\u2192      reserved:  this._reservations.size,\n   290\u2192      occupied:  this._occupants.size,\n   291\u2192      available: this.capacity - this._reservations.size - this._occupants.size,\n   292\u2192    };\n   293\u2192  }\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192  _resetAutoTimer() {\n   296\u2192    const s = this.states[this.state];\n   297\u2192    this.autoTimer = s?.auto_next ? s.auto_next.after * 1000 : 0;\n   298\u2192  }\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192  /**\n   301\u2192   * Trigger an interaction action (e.g. 'use').\n   302\u2192   * @returns {{ objectId, state, speech, emoji }|null}\n   303\u2192   */\n   304\u2192  interact(action = 'use') {\n   305\u2192    const s  = this.states[this.state];\n   306\u2192    const tr = s?.transitions?.[action];\n   307\u2192    if (!tr) return null;\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192    this.state = tr.next;\n   310\u2192    this._resetAutoTimer();\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192    const ns = this.states[this.state];\n   313\u2192    return {\n   314\u2192      objectId: this.id,\n   315\u2192      state:    this.state,\n   316\u2192      speech:   ns?.speech || null,\n   317\u2192      emoji:    ns?.emoji  || null,\n   318\u2192    };\n   319\u2192  }\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192  /**\n   322\u2192   * Advance auto_next timers.\n   323\u2192   * @returns {{ objectId, state, emoji, auto: true }|null}\n   324\u2192   */\n   325\u2192  tick(deltaMs) {\n   326\u2192    if (this.autoTimer <= 0) return null;\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192    this.autoTimer -= deltaMs;\n   329\u2192    if (this.autoTimer > 0) return null;\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192    const s = this.states[this.state];\n   332\u2192    if (!s?.auto_next) return null;\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192    this.state = s.auto_next.state;\n   335\u2192    this._resetAutoTimer();\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192    const ns = this.states[this.state];\n   338\u2192    return {\n   339\u2192      objectId: this.id,\n   340\u2192      state:    this.state,\n   341\u2192      emoji:    ns?.emoji || null,\n   342\u2192      auto:     true,\n   343\u2192    };\n   344\u2192  }\n   345\u2192}\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192// \u2500\u2500 ObjectEngine \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192export class ObjectEngine {\n   350\u2192  constructor() {\n   351\u2192    this._map = new Map(); // id \u2192 ObjectInstance\n   352\u2192  }\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192  addObjects(defs) {\n   355\u2192    for (const def of defs) {\n   356\u2192      this._map.set(def.id, new ObjectInstance(def));\n   357\u2192    }\n   358\u2192  }\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192  /**\n   361\u2192   * Tick all object timers. Returns any events that fired this frame.\n   362\u2192   * @returns {Array<{ objectId, state, emoji, auto }>}\n   363\u2192   */\n   364\u2192  tick(deltaMs) {\n   365\u2192    const events = [];\n   366\u2192    for (const obj of this._map.values()) {\n   367\u2192      const ev = obj.tick(deltaMs);\n   368\u2192      if (ev) events.push(ev);\n   369\u2192    }\n   370\u2192    return events;\n   371\u2192  }\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192  /**\n   374\u2192   * Trigger an action on an object (e.g. 'use').\n   375\u2192   * @returns {{ objectId, state, speech, emoji }|null}\n   376\u2192   */\n   377\u2192  interact(objectId, action = 'use') {\n   378\u2192    return this._map.get(objectId)?.interact(action) ?? null;\n   379\u2192  }\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  getAll()    { return Array.from(this._map.values()); }\n   382\u2192  getById(id) { return this._map.get(id) ?? null; }\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  reserve(objectId, charName) { return this._map.get(objectId)?.reserve(charName) ?? false; }\n   385\u2192  arrive(objectId, charName)  { this._map.get(objectId)?.arrive(charName); }\n   386\u2192  release(objectId, charName) { this._map.get(objectId)?.release(charName); }\n   387\u2192  clearSlots(objectId)        { this._map.get(objectId)?.clearSlots(); }\n   388\u2192  clearAllSlots()             { for (const obj of this._map.values()) obj.clearSlots(); }\n   389\u2192}\n   390\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #47
Now I see the complete picture. Let me trace through the fallback flow: 1. Character tries to `interact()` with an object 2. The `interact()` method calls `s?.transitions?.[action]` where `s = this.states[this.state]` 3. If no transition exists for the action, it returns null 4. `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` is called and pushes a fallback event So the fallback happens when an object doesn't have a 'use' transition in its current state (e.g., if the object is in `brewing` state, there's no `use` transition).But the issue says characters "redirect when targets are full". Looking at the reservation system, when `isFull()` returns true (meaning `_reservations.size + _occupants.size >= capacity`), a character reserves before walking. When they arrive and call `interact()`, if another character has already transitioned the object to a state without a 'use' transition like `brewing`, the arriving character gets null back and triggers the fallback. So the redirect happens when the object becomes unavailable mid-interaction, not just when it's full. wait, both reserved, so tea_set is "full" after B's reservation 4. Character A wouldn't have been able to reserve (it was already full with B's reservation) 5. Unless... character A reserved before B... Hmm, but wait. Look at the scenario: The object has `capacity: 1`. Character A reserves it. Character B tries to use it during the wander - `obj.isFull()` is true (because A has a reservation), so B won't try to use it. Then A arrives. They call `interact()` and the object transitions to `brewing`. A releases reservation, adds to occupants. A starts interacting. So when would `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` get called? Only if `interact()` returns null. This happens when the object's current state doesn't have a 'use' transition. For example, if the tea_set auto-transitions through its states while A is walking to it—going from `idle` to `brewing` to `ready`—and A arrives while it's stuck in `brewing`, then `interact()` would return null since that state has no 'use' transition defined. That's when the fallback kicks in. But here's the thing: A reserved the slot when the object was in `idle`, so the reservation system should still recognize A's claim regardless of what state the object is currently in. Now I need to figure out why the fallback event isn't showing up in the logs. Let me look at how the tick loop actually processes these interactions. The debug loop pulls pending events from the simulation engine and filters for conversation, rejection, and fallback events to add them to the log. But I should check if index.html has a similar event handling setup, or if there's something preventing the fallback event from being queued in the first place.

When they arrive and call `interact()`, if another character has already transitioned the object to a state without a 'use' transition like `brewing`, the arriving character gets null back and triggers the fallback. So the redirect happens when the object becomes unavailable mid-interaction, not just when it's full. wait, both reserved, so tea_set is \"full\" after B's reservation\n4. Character A wouldn't have been able to reserve (it was already full with B's reservation)\n5. Unless... character A reserved before B...\n\nHmm, but wait. Look at the scenario: The object has `capacity: 1`. Character A reserves it. Character B tries to use it during the wander - `obj.isFull()` is true (because A has a reservation), so B won't try to use it.\n\nThen A arrives. They call `interact()` and the object transitions to `brewing`. A releases reservation, adds to occupants. A starts interacting.\n\nSo when would `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` get called?      1→<!DOCTYPE html>
     2→<html lang="en">
     3→<head>
     4→  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
     5→  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
     6→  <title>AI Aquarium — Maple's Café</title>
     7→  <style>
     8→    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
     9→    html, body { width: 100%; height: 100%; background: #1a1a2e; overflow: hidden; }
    10→    canvas { image-rendering: pixelated; image-rendering: crisp-edges; display: block; }
    11→
    12→    /* ── Debug toggle button ── */
    13→    #debug-toggle {
    14→      position: fixed;
    15→      top: 10px;
    16→      right: 10px;
    17→      z-index: 100;
    18→      padding: 4px 10px;
    19→      background: #222240;
    20→      color: #e0ddd4;
    21→      border: 1px solid #444;
    22→      border-radius: 3px;
    23→      cursor: pointer;
    24→      font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace;
    25→      font-size: 11px;
    26→      opacity: 0.75;
    27→      transition: opacity 0.15s;
    28→    }
    29→    #debug-toggle:hover { opacity: 1; background: #2a2a5a; }
    30→    #debug-toggle.open  { opacity: 1; border-color: #6a9a8a; color: #9eebd4; background: #1e2e3e; }
    31→
    32→    /* ── Debug overlay panel ── */
    33→    #debug-panel {
    34→      position: fixed;
    35→      top: 0;
    36→      right: 0;
    37→      width: 280px;
    38→      height: 100%;
    39→      background: #12121e;
    40→      border-left: 1px solid #2a2a44;
    41→      z-index: 99;
    42→      display: none;
    43→      flex-direction: column;
    44→      font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace;
    45→      font-size: 12px;
    46→      color: #e0ddd4;
    47→      overflow: hidden;
    48→    }
    49→    #debug-panel.visible { display: flex; }
    50→
    51→    #debug-panel-header {
    52→      padding: 8px 12px;
    53→      background: #1a1a2e;
    54→      border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a44;
    55→      display: flex;
    56→      align-items: center;
    57→      justify-content: space-between;
    58→      flex-shrink: 0;
    59→    }
    60→    #debug-panel-header span { color: #ffe8a3; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }
    61→    #debug-clock { color: #ffe8a3; font-size: 12px; }
    62→
    63→    #debug-panel-body {
    64→      flex: 1;
    65→      overflow-y: auto;
    66→      padding: 8px;
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    /* scrollbar */
    70→    #debug-panel-body::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 4px; }
    71→    #debug-panel-body::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: #111; }
    72→    #debug-panel-body::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: #333; border-radius: 2px; }
    73→
    74→    .dp-obj {
    75→      display: flex;
    76→      align-items: center;
    77→      justify-content: space-between;
    78→      padding: 5px 6px;
    79→      border-radius: 3px;
    80→      margin-bottom: 3px;
    81→      background: #1a1a2e;
    82→      border: 1px solid #2a2a3e;
    83→      gap: 6px;
    84→    }
    85→    .dp-obj-name  { color: #c8d4e8; flex: 1; text-transform: capitalize; }
    86→    .dp-badge {
    87→      padding: 1px 7px;
    88→      border-radius: 2px;
    89→      font-size: 10px;
    90→      white-space: nowrap;
    91→    }
    92→    .dp-badge.idle   { background: #1a2a1a; color: #77cc77; }
    93→    .dp-badge.active { background: #2a2a1a; color: #cccc77; }
    94→    .dp-badge.done   { background: #1a1a2a; color: #88aacc; }
    95→    .dp-badge.other  { background: #2a1a2a; color: #cc88cc; }
    96→
    97→    .dp-timer {
    98→      height: 2px;
    99→      background: #2a2a40;
   100→      margin-top: 2px;
   101→      margin-bottom: 1px;
   102→      border-radius: 1px;
   103→      overflow: hidden;
   104→    }
   105→    .dp-timer-fill { height: 100%; background: #6a8a5a; border-radius: 1px; transition: width 0.1s ease-out; }
   106→
   107→    .dp-btn {
   108→      padding: 2px 8px;
   109→      font-size: 10px;
   110→      background: #2a3a5a;
   111→      color: #e0ddd4;
   112→      border: 1px solid #3a4a6a;
   113→      border-radius: 2px;
   114→      cursor: pointer;
   115→      font-family: inherit;
   116→      white-space: nowrap;
   117→    }
   118→    .dp-btn:hover { background: #3a4a7a; }
   119→
   120→    .dp-section-title {
   121→      font-size: 10px;
   122→      color: #555;
   123→      text-transform: uppercase;
   124→      letter-spacing: 0.1em;
   125→      padding: 6px 0 4px;
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    #debug-panel-footer {
   129→      padding: 6px 12px;
   130→      border-top: 1px solid #2a2a44;
   131→      flex-shrink: 0;
   132→      text-align: center;
   133→    }
   134→    #debug-panel-footer a {
   135→      color: #6a8aaa;
   136→      font-size: 11px;
   137→      text-decoration: none;
   138→    }
   139→    #debug-panel-footer a:hover { color: #9abacc; text-decoration: underline; }
   140→  </style>
   141→</head>
   142→<body>
   143→<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/phaser@3.88.2/dist/phaser.min.js"></script>
   144→<script type="module">
   145→import { CafeScene } from './src/scene.js?v=15';
   146→new Phaser.Game({
   147→  type: Phaser.AUTO,
   148→  width: 912, height: 642,
   149→  backgroundColor: '#1a1a2e',
   150→  scale: { mode: Phaser.Scale.FIT, autoCenter: Phaser.Scale.CENTER_BOTH },
   151→  render: { antialias: false, pixelArt: true },
   152→  scene: CafeScene,
   153→});
   154→</script>
   155→
   156→<!-- ── Debug toggle button ── -->
   157→<button id="debug-toggle">Debug</button>
   158→
   159→<!-- ── Debug overlay panel ── -->
   160→<div id="debug-panel">
   161→  <div id="debug-panel-header">
   162→    <span>OBJECT STATES</span>
   163→    <span id="debug-clock">—</span>
   164→  </div>
   165→  <div id="debug-panel-body">
   166→    <div id="dp-objects"></div>
   167→  </div>
   168→  <div id="debug-panel-footer">
   169→    <a href="debug.html" target="_blank">&#8599; Full dashboard</a>
   170→  </div>
   171→</div>
   172→
   173→<script>
   174→(function() {
   175→  const toggle  = document.getElementById('debug-toggle');
   176→  const panel   = document.getElementById('debug-panel');
   177→  const dpObjs  = document.getElementById('dp-objects');
   178→  const dpClock = document.getElementById('debug-clock');
   179→
   180→  let open = false;
   181→  let rafId = null;
   182→
   183→  const ACTIVE_STATES = new Set([
   184→    'cooking','brewing','painting','working','tending','reading','on',
   185→  ]);
   186→  const DONE_STATES = new Set(['done','ready','open','cozy','resting']);
   187→
   188→  function badgeCls(state) {
   189→    if (state === 'idle')          return 'idle';
   190→    if (DONE_STATES.has(state))    return 'done';
   191→    if (ACTIVE_STATES.has(state))  return 'active';
   192→    return 'other';
   193→  }
   194→
   195→  // Build DOM for each object row once, then update in place
   196→  let built = false;
   197→
   198→  function buildRows(objects) {
   199→    dpObjs.innerHTML = '';
   200→    for (const obj of objects) {
   201→      const row = document.createElement('div');
   202→      row.innerHTML = `
   203→        <div class="dp-obj" id="dpo-${obj.id}">
   204→          <span class="dp-obj-name">${obj.name}</span>
   205→          <span class="dp-badge" id="dpbadge-${obj.id}"></span>
   206→          <span id="dptimer-${obj.id}"></span>
   207→        </div>
   208→        <div class="dp-timer" id="dptbar-${obj.id}" style="display:none">
   209→          <div class="dp-timer-fill" id="dptfill-${obj.id}" style="width:0%"></div>
   210→        </div>
   211→      `;
   212→      dpObjs.appendChild(row);
   213→    }
   214→    built = true;
   215→  }
   216→
   217→  function updateRows(engine) {
   218→    const objects = engine.objectEngine.getAll();
   219→    if (!built) buildRows(objects);
   220→
   221→    dpClock.textContent = engine.getSimTimeString();
   222→
   223→    for (const obj of objects) {
   224→      const badge = document.getElementById(`dpbadge-${obj.id}`);
   225→      const tbar  = document.getElementById(`dptbar-${obj.id}`);
   226→      const tfill = document.getElementById(`dptfill-${obj.id}`);
   227→      const tspan = document.getElementById(`dptimer-${obj.id}`);
   228→      if (!badge) continue;
   229→
   230→      // Badge
   231→      if (badge.dataset.state !== obj.state) {
   232→        badge.textContent    = obj.state;
   233→        badge.className      = `dp-badge ${badgeCls(obj.state)}`;
   234→        badge.dataset.state  = obj.state;
   235→
   236→        // Rebuild use button
   237→        const existing = tspan.previousSibling;
   238→        // We rebuild via innerHTML of tspan for the button
   239→        const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   240→        const actions  = Object.keys(stateDef?.transitions || {});
   241→        tspan.innerHTML = actions.map(a =>
   242→          `<button class="dp-btn" onclick="window._dpInteract('${obj.id}','${a}')">${a}</button>`
   243→        ).join('');
   244→      }
   245→
   246→      // Timer bar
   247→      const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];
   248→      const hasTimer = !!(stateDef?.auto_next && obj.autoTimer > 0);
   249→      if (hasTimer) {
   250→        const totalMs = stateDef.auto_next.after * 1000;
   251→        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - obj.autoTimer / totalMs) * 100));
   252→        tbar.style.display  = '';
   253→        tfill.style.width   = `${pct.toFixed(1)}%`;
   254→      } else {
   255→        tbar.style.display = 'none';
   256→      }
   257→    }
   258→  }
   259→
   260→  window._dpInteract = function(id, action) {
   261→    if (!window._gameEngine) return;
   262→    window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact(id, action);
   263→  };
   264→
   265→  function renderLoop() {
   266→    if (!open) { rafId = null; return; }
   267→    if (window._gameEngine) updateRows(window._gameEngine);
   268→    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(renderLoop);
   269→  }
   270→
   271→  toggle.addEventListener('click', () => {
   272→    open = !open;
   273→    toggle.classList.toggle('open', open);
   274→    toggle.textContent = open ? 'Debug ✕' : 'Debug';
   275→    panel.classList.toggle('visible', open);
   276→    if (open && !rafId) {
   277→      built = false; // rebuild rows on open in case engine wasn't ready before
   278→      rafId = requestAnimationFrame(renderLoop);
   279→    }
   280→  });
   281→})();
   282→</script>
   283→</body>
   284→</html>
   285→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

 238\u2192        // We rebuild via innerHTML of tspan for the button\n   239\u2192        const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];\n   240\u2192        const actions  = Object.keys(stateDef?.transitions || {});\n   241\u2192        tspan.innerHTML = actions.map(a =>\n   242\u2192          `<button class=\"dp-btn\" onclick=\"window._dpInteract('${obj.id}','${a}')\">${a}</button>`\n   243\u2192        ).join('');\n   244\u2192      }\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192      // Timer bar\n   247\u2192      const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];\n   248\u2192      const hasTimer = !!(stateDef?.auto_next && obj.autoTimer > 0);\n   249\u2192      if (hasTimer) {\n   250\u2192        const totalMs = stateDef.auto_next.after * 1000;\n   251\u2192        const pct = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (1 - obj.autoTimer / totalMs) * 100));\n   252\u2192        tbar.style.display  = '';\n   253\u2192        tfill.style.width   = `${pct.toFixed(1)}%`;\n   254\u2192      } else {\n   255\u2192        tbar.style.display = 'none';\n   256\u2192      }\n   257\u2192    }\n   258\u2192  }\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192  window._dpInteract = function(id, action) {\n   261\u2192    if (!window._gameEngine) return;\n   262\u2192    window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact(id, action);\n   263\u2192  };\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  function renderLoop() {\n   266\u2192    if (!open) { rafId = null; return; }\n   267\u2192    if (window._gameEngine) updateRows(window._gameEngine);\n   268\u2192    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(renderLoop);\n   269\u2192  }\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192  toggle.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   272\u2192    open = !open;\n   273\u2192    toggle.classList.toggle('open', open);\n   274\u2192    toggle.textContent = open ? 'Debug \u2715' : 'Debug';\n   275\u2192    panel.classList.toggle('visible', open);\n   276\u2192    if (open && !rafId) {\n   277\u2192      built = false; // rebuild rows on open in case engine wasn't ready before\n   278\u2192      rafId = requestAnimationFrame(renderLoop);\n   279\u2192    }\n   280\u2192  });\n   281\u2192})();\n   282\u2192</script>\n   283\u2192</body>\n   284\u2192</html>\n   285\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

    "signature": "EsMCCkYICxgCKkAYW7GjMXt+om+QMhvBUesjvT8iCaBSin55FzoHw6bMqgZ5MiYq6SOfQpc2vi5E4FasUncJ09H1ArWBd6gKxruhEgxxgQEbcth/9QP32tUaDG+QwJ3aJNmWU0LlgCIwrSTI2IAwg7JVXzuILFNQdi/GjamgGrRQElNFYatyxnicGObl5tRvbH6rAihtR4m1KqoBSE5bJYbR52NNT/gtIV/VqIYUoppUpn+5HemM3YyIP1cpL7Axxq5r8peohVyZdvuHElT2iag1HM3Zl2uSpYKkeushrgIG2FF4Tn8uBd9EZaNHhOhl+vvNB8iK/7f/s1T6i8HH2/pJ+u+IKWN42OzA/9cMqtrfQkI5KGSVXvdi6LeeRUYJsNnENFhxdyxFP1FHjztzN5K2CBPpOV6/ZkfsU3/AwxSHlfPM8cMYAQ=="

USERMESSAGE #52
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→import { Character }         from './Character.js';
     2→import { SimulationEngine } from './Engine.js';
     3→import { AIEngine }          from './AIEngine.js';
     4→import { showSpeech }        from './speechBubble.js';
     5→import { OBJECT_DEFS }       from './objects.js';
     6→
     7→// ─── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8→
     9→const TILE_SIZE  = 48;
    10→const GRID_COLS  = 19;
    11→const GRID_ROWS  = 13;
    12→
    13→// Walk spritesheet layout (2304×864, 48 cols × 9 rows, frame size 48×96)
    14→// Frame number = row * 48 + col.  Verified by visual inspection:
    15→//   Row 0 → up   (4 frames:  0–3)
    16→//   Row 3 → right (6 frames: 144–149)
    17→//   Row 6 → down  (6 frames: 288–293)
    18→// LEFT reuses the RIGHT frames with flipX applied at runtime.
    19→const WALK_DIRS = [
    20→  { key: 'up',    start:   0, end:   3 },
    21→  { key: 'down',  start: 288, end: 293 },
    22→  { key: 'right', start: 144, end: 149 },
    23→  { key: 'left',  start: 144, end: 149 }, // mirrored via flipX
    24→];
    25→
    26→// Character definitions — tile positions within walkable area (col 1–17, row 2–9)
    27→const CHARS = [
    28→  {
    29→    name: 'Amelia', label: 'Maple', tx: 6, ty: 7,
    30→    dialogue: ['Perfect brew today~', 'Want some tea?', 'The kitchen smells amazing'],
    31→  },
    32→  {
    33→    name: 'Lucy', label: 'Sol', tx: 10, ty: 6,
    34→    dialogue: ['The bonsai looks healthy', 'I should water the plants', 'Such peaceful light...'],
    35→  },
    36→  {
    37→    name: 'Ash', label: 'Nyx', tx: 13, ty: 5,
    38→    dialogue: ['I need more paint...', 'This light is perfect for sketching', 'Where did I put my brush?'],
    39→  },
    40→];
    41→
    42→// ─── Walkable grid ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→// 19 cols × 13 rows.
    44→// Interior floor (main room): cols 1–17, rows 2–9.
    45→// Rows 0–1 = roof/top wall; rows 10–12 = genkan / exterior — not walkable.
    46→
    47→function buildWalkableGrid() {
    48→  const grid = [];
    49→  for (let row = 0; row < GRID_ROWS; row++) {
    50→    const r = [];
    51→    for (let col = 0; col < GRID_COLS; col++) {
    52→      r.push(col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9);
    53→    }
    54→    grid.push(r);
    55→  }
    56→  // Mark tiles occupied by objects as non-walkable
    57→  for (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {
    58→    const { tx, ty } = def.position;
    59→    if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) {
    60→      grid[ty][tx] = false;
    61→    }
    62→  }
    63→  return grid;
    64→}
    65→
    66→// ─── Scene ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67→
    68→export class CafeScene extends Phaser.Scene {
    69→  constructor() { super({ key: 'CafeScene' }); }
    70→
    71→  // ── Preload ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72→
    73→  preload() {
    74→    this.load.image('jp', 'assets/designs/Japanese_Home_1_preview_48x48.png');
    75→
    76→    ['Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash'].forEach(name => {
    77→      this.load.spritesheet(`${name}_idle`, `assets/characters/${name}_idle.png`, {
    78→        frameWidth: 48, frameHeight: 96,
    79→      });
    80→      this.load.spritesheet(`${name}_walk`, `assets/characters/${name}_walk.png`, {
    81→        frameWidth: 48, frameHeight: 96,
    82→      });
    83→    });
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  // ── Create ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    87→
    88→  create() {
    89→    // Background
    90→    const img = this.add.image(0, 0, 'jp').setOrigin(0, 0);
    91→    this.cameras.main.setBounds(0, 0, img.width, img.height);
    92→    this.cameras.main.centerOn(img.width / 2, img.height / 2);
    93→
    94→    // Animations
    95→    ['Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash'].forEach(name => {
    96→      this.anims.create({
    97→        key: `${name}_idle_anim`,
    98→        frames: this.anims.generateFrameNumbers(`${name}_idle`, { start: 0, end: 5 }),
    99→        frameRate: 6,
   100→        repeat: -1,
   101→      });
   102→
   103→      WALK_DIRS.forEach(({ key, start, end }) => {
   104→        this.anims.create({
   105→          key: `${name}_walk_${key}`,
   106→          frames: this.anims.generateFrameNumbers(`${name}_walk`, { start, end }),
   107→          frameRate: 8,
   108→          repeat: -1,
   109→        });
   110→      });
   111→    });
   112→
   113→    // Engine
   114→    const walkable = buildWalkableGrid();
   115→    this.engine = new SimulationEngine({
   116→      walkable,
   117→      gridCols: GRID_COLS,
   118→      gridRows: GRID_ROWS,
   119→      aiEngine: new AIEngine({
   120→        endpoint: '/api/llm',
   121→        model: 'anthropic/claude-3-haiku',
   122→        decisionIntervalMs: 30_000,
   123→      }),
   124→    });
   125→
   126→    // Characters
   127→    CHARS.forEach(({ name, label, tx, ty, dialogue }) => {
   128→      const initPx = tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;
   129→      const initPy = (ty + 1) * TILE_SIZE;
   130→
   131→      const sprite = this.add.sprite(initPx, initPy, `${name}_idle`)
   132→        .setOrigin(0.5, 1)
   133→        .setDepth(10);
   134→      sprite.play(`${name}_idle_anim`);
   135→
   136→      const nameLabel = this.add.text(initPx, initPy - 100, label, {
   137→        fontSize: '12px',
   138→        color: '#ffffff',
   139→        fontFamily: 'monospace',
   140→        stroke: '#000000',
   141→        strokeThickness: 2,
   142→      }).setOrigin(0.5, 1).setDepth(11);
   143→
   144→      const char = new Character({
   145→        name, label, tx, ty, sprite, nameLabel,
   146→        walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS,
   147→        dialogue,
   148→      });
   149→
   150→      this.engine.addCharacter(char);
   151→    });
   152→
   153→    // Expose engine for the debug overlay panel
   154→    window._gameEngine = this.engine;
   155→
   156→    // Sim clock display (screen-fixed, top-left)
   157→    this.clockText = this.add.text(8, 8, '', {
   158→      fontSize: '13px',
   159→      color: '#ffe8a3',
   160→      fontFamily: 'monospace',
   161→      stroke: '#000000',
   162→      strokeThickness: 2,
   163→    }).setDepth(20).setScrollFactor(0);
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  // ── Update ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   167→
   168→  update(_time, delta) {
   169→    this.engine.update(delta);
   170→    this.clockText.setText(this.engine.getSimTimeString());
   171→
   172→    // Show speech bubbles queued by the engine
   173→    for (const char of this.engine.characters) {
   174→      if (char.pendingSpeech && char.sprite) {
   175→        showSpeech(this, char.sprite, char.pendingSpeech);
   176→        char.pendingSpeech = null;
   177→      }
   178→    }
   179→
   180→    // Float emoji above objects that completed a transition
   181→    if (this.engine.pendingEvents.length > 0) {
   182→      for (const ev of this.engine.pendingEvents) {
   183→        if (!ev.emoji) continue;
   184→        const obj = this.engine.objectEngine.getById(ev.objectId);
   185→        if (!obj) continue;
   186→        const px = obj.tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;
   187→        const py = obj.ty * TILE_SIZE;
   188→        const label = this.add.text(px, py, ev.emoji, { fontSize: '24px' }).setDepth(25);
   189→        this.tweens.add({
   190→          targets: label,
   191→          y:       label.y - 40,
   192→          alpha:   0,
   193→          duration: 2000,
   194→          onComplete: () => label.destroy(),
   195→        });
   196→      }
   197→      this.engine.pendingEvents = [];
   198→    }
   199→  }
   200→}
   201→

2;\n   129\u2192      const initPy = (ty + 1) * TILE_SIZE;\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192      const sprite = this.add.sprite(initPx, initPy, `${name}_idle`)\n   132\u2192        .setOrigin(0.5, 1)\n   133\u2192        .setDepth(10);\n   134\u2192      sprite.play(`${name}_idle_anim`);\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192      const nameLabel = this.add.text(initPx, initPy - 100, label, {\n   137\u2192        fontSize: '12px',\n   138\u2192        color: '#ffffff',\n   139\u2192        fontFamily: 'monospace',\n   140\u2192        stroke: '#000000',\n   141\u2192        strokeThickness: 2,\n   142\u2192      }).setOrigin(0.5, 1).setDepth(11);\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192      const char = new Character({\n   145\u2192        name, label, tx, ty, sprite, nameLabel,\n   146\u2192        walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS,\n   147\u2192        dialogue,\n   148\u2192      });\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192      this.engine.addCharacter(char);\n   151\u2192    });\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    // Expose engine for the debug overlay panel\n   154\u2192    window._gameEngine = this.engine;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192    // Sim clock display (screen-fixed, top-left)\n   157\u2192    this.clockText = this.add.text(8, 8, '', {\n   158\u2192      fontSize: '13px',\n   159\u2192      color: '#ffe8a3',\n   160\u2192      fontFamily: 'monospace',\n   161\u2192      stroke: '#000000',\n   162\u2192      strokeThickness: 2,\n   163\u2192    }).setDepth(20).setScrollFactor(0);\n   164\u2192  }\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 Update \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192  update(_time, delta) {\n   169\u2192    this.engine.update(delta);\n   170\u2192    this.clockText.setText(this.engine.getSimTimeString());\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    // Show speech bubbles queued by the engine\n   173\u2192    for (const char of this.engine.characters) {\n   174\u2192      if (char.pendingSpeech && char.sprite) {\n   175\u2192        showSpeech(this, char.sprite, char.pendingSpeech);\n   176\u2192        char.pendingSpeech = null;\n   177\u2192      }\n   178\u2192    }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    // Float emoji above objects that completed a transition\n   181\u2192    if (this.engine.pendingEvents.length > 0) {\n   182\u2192      for (const ev of this.engine.pendingEvents) {\n   183\u2192        if (!ev.emoji) continue;\n   184\u2192        const obj = this.engine.objectEngine.getById(ev.objectId);\n   185\u2192        if (!obj) continue;\n   186\u2192        const px = obj.tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2;\n   187\u2192        const py = obj.ty * TILE_SIZE;\n   188\u2192        const label = this.add.text(px, py, ev.emoji, { fontSize: '24px' }).setDepth(25);\n   189\u2192        this.tweens.add({\n   190\u2192          targets: label,\n   191\u2192          y:       label.y - 40,\n   192\u2192          alpha:   0,\n   193\u2192          duration: 2000,\n   194\u2192          onComplete: () => label.destroy(),\n   195\u2192        });\n   196\u2192      }\n   197\u2192      this.engine.pendingEvents = [];\n   198\u2192    }\n   199\u2192  }\n   200\u2192}\n   201\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #53
I found the bug! Look at `scene.js` lines 181-198: ```javascript // Float emoji above objects that completed a transition if (this.engine.pendingEvents.length > 0) { for (const ev of this.engine.pendingEvents) { if (!ev.emoji) continue; const obj = this.engine.objectEngine.getById(ev.objectId); if (!obj) continue; const px = obj.tx * TILE_SIZE + TILE_SIZE / 2; const py = obj.ty * TILE_SIZE; const label = this.add.text(px, py, ev.emoji, { fontSize: '24px' }).setDepth(25); ``` The scene loops through pending events to create floating emoji animations, then clears the entire array afterward. But wait, `debug.html` creates its own instance, so... Still writing animation code... its own `simEngine` instance, separate from `scene.js`. So these are completely different instances. Let me re-read... Yes! Looking at debug.html, it creates its own `SimulationEngine` with the walkable grid and AI engine, while scene.js creates a separate instance. So when `scene.js` clears its own `engine.pendingEvents`, it wouldn't affect debug.html's `simEngine.pendingEvents`. Now let me trace through the debug.html tick loop to see where the actual bug is. The loop consumes pending events from the simulation engine, filtering for conversation starts, rejections, and fallbacks, then logs them with timestamps. The key question is whether `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` is actually being triggered in debug.html. Let me walk through the sequence: a character decides to interact with an object, reserves a slot and walks there, the object might auto-transition during the walk, the character arrives and calls `_handleObjectArrival`, which calls `objectEngine.interact()`, and if that returns null because the object has no 'use' transition, then `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` gets invoked. But the issue mentions characters DO redirect when targets are full, so I need to consider the case where a character reserves a slot but the object becomes unavailable. Most objects have `capacity: 1`, so if object A is already in the `brewing` state with an occupant, there's no room for another character. The tricky part is that character B could have reserved the slot before character A started using it—B reserves when the object is idle and available, but then A also reserves and starts using it before B arrives. Actually, I think the real issue might be with the debug buttons bypassing the reservation system entirely. If a character manually triggers a state transition like `_dbInteract('tea_set', 'use')` through the debug interface, it could move the object to `brewing` state without going through the slot reservation logic, leaving the object occupied but with zero reserved slots. Then when another character's wander picks up that same object, it looks available (capacity=1, no occupants registered in the slot system, no reservations), but when they arrive and try to interact, the object is already in `brewing` state with no 'use' transition available, triggering the fallback. The pattern seems to be that objects auto-transition to intermediate states like `brewing`, `painting`, or `cooking` that don't have 'use' transitions, which would cause the fallback to trigger whenever a character arrives at an object mid-transition. The reservation system with capacity=1 prevents double-booking, so B would see the object is full and skip it after A reserves. The fallback to an alternative object would only trigger if the tea_set auto-transitioned to a state without a 'use' transition while A is walking, but since idle has no auto_next, this scenario shouldn't actually occur in normal operation. Now I'm tracing through the actual race condition: Nyx's wander check fires when bonsai appears available, but there's a timing gap before Nyx actually reserves it—during that window, Maple could claim it, leaving Nyx unable to reserve and forcing a redirect to an alternative object. Writing interaction logic... The wander logic safely reserves objects before targeting them, checking both capacity and valid transitions. The AI engine is different though—it makes async decisions, so there's a window where the game state could change between when the AI evaluates an object as available and when it actually applies that decision to the character. Let me trace through a concrete scenario: Maple uses the bonsai, then Nyx's AI fires and decides to interact with it. By the time `_applyDecision` runs, the bonsai is full, so the guard check `if (!obj.isFull() && stateDef?.transitions?.use)` should catch this and prevent the interaction. But I need to verify when `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` actually gets triggered in practice. Actually, I think I see the issue now—the AI engine is asynchronous. Nyx's LLM request fires in the background, Maple occupies the bonsai in the meantime, and then when the response comes back with "interact bonsai," `_applyDecision` runs against a now-full object. The guard check should prevent assignment, but something's allowing Nyx to target it anyway. Let me look at the evidence again—it says Nyx "targets bonsai while Maple is using it," which suggests either the AI decision was made before Maple started using it, or there's a timing issue with how the reservation system checks occupancy. Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Maybe the AI engine has a bug where it sends a character to interact with an object that's already at capacity. Let me check the interaction handler... Examining the handler logic... So there's a guard in place: the code checks if the object isn't full before attempting to reserve it, and only sets the character's target and pending action if the reservation succeeds. But there's a race condition here—what if `moveTo()` completes but `reserve()` fails because the object filled up in between those two calls? The character would be walking toward the object with no valid reservation. When the character arrives at the destination, they'd have no `targetObjectId` set, so the arrival handler wouldn't trigger. They'd just go idle and eventually wander off. That's not the issue though, since the fallback behavior is actually working—characters are redirecting. The real question is why the fallback events aren't showing up in the logs when the redirect happens. Looking at `_fallbackToAlternativeObject`, it always pushes a fallback event, so either the function isn't being called at all, or the event is getting lost somewhere. Let me trace through other code paths that could cause a redirect without emitting that event. In `_handleObjectArrival`, when the character successfully interacts with an object, the code handles the interaction result and manages the character's state... Examining the else branch - if `interact()` returns null (meaning no valid transition, perhaps because the object changed state during the walk), the code releases the reservation and calls fallback. But what if the object is actually full by the time the character arrives, even though they had a valid reservation? Let me check the `interact()` method to see how it handles that scenario. Looking at the implementation, `interact()` only returns null if there's no transition defined for the action - it doesn't validate slot availability. So the slot system is purely a reservation mechanism to prevent multiple characters from targeting the same object, not something that would cause `interact()` to fail at arrival time. That means `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` gets triggered when the state machine doesn't have the expected transition, not when slots are full. But the issue clearly states characters redirect when targets are full, so either the fallback is happening due to a state transition that occurred while the character was walking, or I'm missing something about how two characters competing for the same object through wander logic would interact. So there's no race in same-frame updates. But the AI engine runs first in the update loop, setting timers and making async requests before the character loop processes wandering and arrivals. The `_requestDecision` call is async with `await fetch()`, and `_applyDecision` fires in the `.then()` callback outside the main update—potentially at any frame. This could create a race where Character A's decision applies while Character B is simultaneously reserving the same object. However, since JavaScript is single-threaded and the AI response callback executes between `requestAnimationFrame` calls in the microtask queue rather than during the update itself, the callback actually fires between frames, not during the main loop. I need to step back and focus on the actual problem—fallback events aren't appearing. Let me trace through the `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` method to see if there's a conditional blocking the event emission. The method reserves an alternative object and queues a fallback event with the character and object details, or if no alternative exists, it sets a wander timer and still emits a fallback event with a null target. Both paths should be pushing events to the queue, so the emission logic itself looks sound. Now I'm tracing through when `_handleObjectArrival` actually gets triggered. It requires the character to be idle and have a targetObjectId set, and once it fires, that targetObjectId gets cleared so it only happens once per arrival. The real question is what happens when the character finishes their walk and transitions back to idle—I need to check where that state change occurs.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA
