      "old_string": "      case 'move':\n        if (decision.targetTile) {\n          // Release any pending object reservation before moving freely\n          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n            character.targetObjectId = null;\n          }\n          character.moveTo(decision.targetTile.tx, decision.targetTile.ty);\n        }\n        break;\n\n      case 'interact': {\n        const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId);\n        if (obj) {\n          // If already heading to a different object, release that reservation first\n          if (character.targetObjectId && character.targetObjectId !== obj.id) {\n            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n            character.targetObjectId = null;\n          }\n\n          // Only commit if a slot is available and object is currently interactable\n          const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];\n          if (!obj.isFull() && stateDef?.transitions?.use) {\n            // Walk to an adjacent walkable tile (same pattern as wander engine)\n            const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(obj.tx, obj.ty);\n            if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {\n              if (obj.reserve(character.name)) { // claim slot; guard against any race\n                character.targetObjectId = obj.id;\n                character.pendingAction  = 'use';\n              }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n\n      case 'idle':\n        // Release any pending reservation, then stay put for one full decision interval\n        if (character.targetObjectId) {\n          const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n          if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n          character.targetObjectId = null;\n        }\n        character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;\n        break;\n\n      case 'talk': {\n        const target = characters.find(c => c.label === decision.targetCharacter);\n        if (target) {\n          // Evaluate Phase: check if B can accept a conversation request.\n          // Accept if idle or walking; reject if interacting, talking, or otherwise busy.\n          const canAccept = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';\n\n          if (!canAccept) {\n            // B is busy \u2014 log the rejection and let A fall back to idle/wander\n            if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n              simState.pushEvent({\n                type:           'talk_rejected',\n                initiatorLabel: character.label,\n                targetLabel:    target.label,\n                reason:         target.action,\n              });\n            }\n            // A idles for the normal decision interval (fallback handled by Engine wander)\n            character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;\n            break;\n          }\n\n          // Release any previous object reservation before walking to talk\n          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n            if (prevObj) prevObj.release(character.name);\n            character.targetObjectId = null;\n          }",
      "new_string": "      case 'move':\n        if (decision.targetTile) {\n          // Release any pending object reservation before moving freely\n          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n            if (prevObj) {\n              prevObj.release(character.name);\n              if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n                simState.pushEvent({\n                  type:      'fallback',\n                  charLabel: character.label,\n                  reason:    'plan_changed',\n                  fromLabel: prevObj.label ?? prevObj.name,\n                  toLabel:   null,\n                });\n              }\n            }\n            character.targetObjectId = null;\n          }\n          character.moveTo(decision.targetTile.tx, decision.targetTile.ty);\n        }\n        break;\n\n      case 'interact': {\n        const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId);\n        if (obj) {\n          // If already heading to a different object, release that reservation first\n          let abandonedLabel = null;\n          if (character.targetObjectId && character.targetObjectId !== obj.id) {\n            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n            if (prevObj) {\n              prevObj.release(character.name);\n              abandonedLabel = prevObj.label ?? prevObj.name;\n            }\n            character.targetObjectId = null;\n          }\n\n          // Only commit if a slot is available and object is currently interactable\n          const stateDef = obj.states[obj.state];\n          if (!obj.isFull() && stateDef?.transitions?.use) {\n            // Walk to an adjacent walkable tile (same pattern as wander engine)\n            const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(obj.tx, obj.ty);\n            if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {\n              if (obj.reserve(character.name)) { // claim slot; guard against any race\n                character.targetObjectId = obj.id;\n                character.pendingAction  = 'use';\n                if (abandonedLabel && simState?.pushEvent) {\n                  simState.pushEvent({\n                    type:      'fallback',\n                    charLabel: character.label,\n                    reason:    'plan_changed',\n                    fromLabel: abandonedLabel,\n                    toLabel:   obj.label ?? obj.name,\n                  });\n                }\n              }\n            }\n          } else if (abandonedLabel && simState?.pushEvent) {\n            // New target unavailable \u2014 character goes idle after abandoning previous\n            simState.pushEvent({\n              type:      'fallback',\n              charLabel: character.label,\n              reason:    'object_unavailable',\n              fromLabel: abandonedLabel,\n              toLabel:   null,\n            });\n          }\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n\n      case 'idle':\n        // Release any pending reservation, then stay put for one full decision interval\n        if (character.targetObjectId) {\n          const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n          if (prevObj) {\n            prevObj.release(character.name);\n            if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n              simState.pushEvent({\n                type:      'fallback',\n                charLabel: character.label,\n                reason:    'plan_changed',\n                fromLabel: prevObj.label ?? prevObj.name,\n                toLabel:   null,\n              });\n            }\n          }\n          character.targetObjectId = null;\n        }\n        character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;\n        break;\n\n      case 'talk': {\n        const target = characters.find(c => c.label === decision.targetCharacter);\n        if (target) {\n          // Evaluate Phase: check if B can accept a conversation request.\n          // Accept if idle or walking; reject if interacting, talking, or otherwise busy.\n          const canAccept = target.action === 'idle' || target.action === 'walking';\n\n          if (!canAccept) {\n            // B is busy \u2014 log the rejection and let A fall back to idle/wander\n            if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n              simState.pushEvent({\n                type:           'talk_rejected',\n                initiatorLabel: character.label,\n                targetLabel:    target.label,\n                reason:         target.action,\n              });\n            }\n            // A idles for the normal decision interval (fallback handled by Engine wander)\n            character.wanderTimer = this.decisionIntervalMs;\n            break;\n          }\n\n          // Release any previous object reservation before walking to talk\n          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n            if (prevObj) {\n              prevObj.release(character.name);\n              if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n                simState.pushEvent({\n                  type:      'fallback',\n                  charLabel: character.label,\n                  reason:    'plan_changed',\n                  fromLabel: prevObj.label ?? prevObj.name,\n                  toLabel:   target.label,\n                });\n              }\n            }\n            character.targetObjectId = null;\n          }"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #80
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   278→          // Release any previous object reservation before walking to talk
   279→          if (character.targetObjectId) {
   280→            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);
   281→            if (prevObj) {
   282→              prevObj.release(character.name);
   283→              if (simState?.pushEvent) {
   284→                simState.pushEvent({
   285→                  type:      'fallback',
   286→                  charLabel: character.label,
   287→                  reason:    'plan_changed',
   288→                  fromLabel: prevObj.label ?? prevObj.name,
   289→                  toLabel:   target.label,
   290→                });
   291→              }
   292→            }
   293→            character.targetObjectId = null;
   294→          }
   295→
   296→          // Request Phase: walk A to a tile adjacent to B.
   297→          // Arrival handler in Engine will start the shared talking state.
   298→          const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty));
   299→          if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {
   300→            // Store pending talk so Engine knows what to do on arrival
   301→            character.pendingTalkTarget   = target;
   302→            character.pendingTalkDialogue = decision.dialogue       || null;
   303→            character.pendingTalkResponse = decision.targetDialogue || null;
   304→          }
   305→        }
   306→        break;
   307→      }
   308→    }
   309→
   310→    // For talk actions, dialogue is held until both characters enter talking state.
   311→    if (decision.dialogue && decision.action !== 'talk') {
   312→      character.currentDialogue = decision.dialogue;
   313→      character.dialogueTimer   = 5000;
   314→    }
   315→
   316→    if (decision.description) {
   317→      console.log(`[${character.label}] ${decision.description}`);
   318→    }
   319→
   320→    if (this.onDecision) {
   321→      this.onDecision(character, decision, objects, simState);
   322→    }
   323→  }
   324→}
   325→

    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01ACBLvcqkgDRgSpevmsQCrz",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "   278\u2192          // Release any previous object reservation before walking to talk\n   279\u2192          if (character.targetObjectId) {\n   280\u2192            const prevObj = objects.find(o => o.id === character.targetObjectId);\n   281\u2192            if (prevObj) {\n   282\u2192              prevObj.release(character.name);\n   283\u2192              if (simState?.pushEvent) {\n   284\u2192                simState.pushEvent({\n   285\u2192                  type:      'fallback',\n   286\u2192                  charLabel: character.label,\n   287\u2192                  reason:    'plan_changed',\n   288\u2192                  fromLabel: prevObj.label ?? prevObj.name,\n   289\u2192                  toLabel:   target.label,\n   290\u2192                });\n   291\u2192              }\n   292\u2192            }\n   293\u2192            character.targetObjectId = null;\n   294\u2192          }\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192          // Request Phase: walk A to a tile adjacent to B.\n   297\u2192          // Arrival handler in Engine will start the shared talking state.\n   298\u2192          const adjTile = simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.(Math.round(target.tx), Math.round(target.ty));\n   299\u2192          if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {\n   300\u2192            // Store pending talk so Engine knows what to do on arrival\n   301\u2192            character.pendingTalkTarget   = target;\n   302\u2192            character.pendingTalkDialogue = decision.dialogue       || null;\n   303\u2192            character.pendingTalkResponse = decision.targetDialogue || null;\n   304\u2192          }\n   305\u2192        }\n   306\u2192        break;\n   307\u2192      }\n   308\u2192    }\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192    // For talk actions, dialogue is held until both characters enter talking state.\n   311\u2192    if (decision.dialogue && decision.action !== 'talk') {\n   312\u2192      character.currentDialogue = decision.dialogue;\n   313\u2192      character.dialogueTimer   = 5000;\n   314\u2192    }\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    if (decision.description) {\n   317\u2192      console.log(`[${character.label}] ${decision.description}`);\n   318\u2192    }\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192    if (this.onDecision) {\n   321\u2192      this.onDecision(character, decision, objects, simState);\n   322\u2192    }\n   323\u2192  }\n   324\u2192}\n   325\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #97
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "node --input-type=module --eval \"\nimport { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';\nimport { AIEngine } from './src/AIEngine.js';\nimport { Character } from './src/Character.js';\nimport { OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';\n\nconst GRID_COLS = 19, GRID_ROWS = 13;\nconst walkable = Array.from({length: GRID_ROWS}, (_, row) =>\n  Array.from({length: GRID_COLS}, (_, col) => col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9));\nfor (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n  const {tx, ty} = def.position;\n  if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) walkable[ty][tx] = false;\n}\n\nconst events = [];\nconst ai = new AIEngine({ endpoint: null });\nconst engine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\nconst char = new Character({ name: 'Amelia', label: 'Maple', tx: 6, ty: 7, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\nengine.addCharacter(char);\n\n// Simulate AI apply decision with existing target\nchar.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\nconst obj_bonsai = engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai');\nobj_bonsai.reserve('Amelia');\n\nconst simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n\n// Apply 'idle' decision \u2014 should emit fallback event\nai._applyDecision(char, { action: 'idle' }, [], engine.objectEngine.getAll(), simState);\n\nconsole.log('Events emitted:', JSON.stringify(events, null, 2));\nconsole.log('Bonsai reservations:', obj_bonsai.getReservations());\nconsole.log('char.targetObjectId:', char.targetObjectId);\n\" 2>&1"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01368qDKJzjv7Lqo349pf56b",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "node --input-type=module --eval \"\nimport { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';\nimport { AIEngine } from './src/AIEngine.js';\nimport { Character } from './src/Character.js';\nimport { OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';\n\nconst GRID_COLS = 19, GRID_ROWS = 13;\nconst walkable = Array.from({length: GRID_ROWS}, (_, row) =>\n  Array.from({length: GRID_COLS}, (_, col) => col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9));\nfor (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n  const {tx, ty} = def.position;\n  if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) walkable[ty][tx] = false;\n}\n\nconst events = [];\nconst ai = new AIEngine({ endpoint: null });\nconst engine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\nconst char = new Character({ name: 'Amelia', label: 'Maple', tx: 6, ty: 7, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\nengine.addCharacter(char);\n\n// Simulate AI apply decision with existing target\nchar.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\nconst obj_bonsai = engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai');\nobj_bonsai.reserve('Amelia');\n\nconst simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n\n// Apply 'idle' decision \u2014 should emit fallback event\nai._applyDecision(char, { action: 'idle' }, [], engine.objectEngine.getAll(), simState);\n\nconsole.log('Events emitted:', JSON.stringify(events, null, 2));\nconsole.log('Bonsai reservations:', obj_bonsai.getReservations());\nconsole.log('char.targetObjectId:', char.targetObjectId);\n\" 2>&1"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #101
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "node --input-type=module --eval \"\nimport { AIEngine } from './src/AIEngine.js';\nimport { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';\nimport { Character } from './src/Character.js';\nimport { OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';\n\nconst GRID_COLS = 19, GRID_ROWS = 13;\nconst walkable = Array.from({length: GRID_ROWS}, (_, row) =>\n  Array.from({length: GRID_COLS}, (_, col) => col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9));\nfor (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n  const {tx, ty} = def.position;\n  if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) walkable[ty][tx] = false;\n}\n\nconst ai = new AIEngine({ endpoint: null });\nconst engine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\n\nfunction makeChar(name, label, tx, ty) {\n  const c = new Character({ name, label, tx, ty, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\n  engine.addCharacter(c);\n  return c;\n}\n\nconst maple = makeChar('Amelia', 'Maple', 6, 7);\nconst sol   = makeChar('Lucy',   'Sol',   10, 6);\nconst objects = engine.objectEngine.getAll();\n\n// Test 1: 'interact' redirect from bonsai \u2192 tea_set\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'interact', targetId: 'tea_set' }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test interact redirect:', JSON.stringify(events));\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\n// Test 2: 'interact' redirect to unavailable object (stove already full)\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('stove').reserve('Lucy'); // make stove full\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'interact', targetId: 'stove' }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test interact \u2192 full (unavailable):', JSON.stringify(events));\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('stove').release('Lucy');\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\n// Test 3: 'talk' redirect from bonsai \u2192 Sol\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'talk', targetCharacter: 'Sol' }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test talk redirect:', JSON.stringify(events));\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\n// Test 4: 'move' redirect from bonsai \u2192 tile\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'move', targetTile: { tx: 5, ty: 5 } }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test move redirect:', JSON.stringify(events));\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\" 2>&1"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {

    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "node --input-type=module --eval \"\nimport { AIEngine } from './src/AIEngine.js';\nimport { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';\nimport { Character } from './src/Character.js';\nimport { OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';\n\nconst GRID_COLS = 19, GRID_ROWS = 13;\nconst walkable = Array.from({length: GRID_ROWS}, (_, row) =>\n  Array.from({length: GRID_COLS}, (_, col) => col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9));\nfor (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n  const {tx, ty} = def.position;\n  if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) walkable[ty][tx] = false;\n}\n\nconst ai = new AIEngine({ endpoint: null });\nconst engine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\n\nfunction makeChar(name, label, tx, ty) {\n  const c = new Character({ name, label, tx, ty, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\n  engine.addCharacter(c);\n  return c;\n}\n\nconst maple = makeChar('Amelia', 'Maple', 6, 7);\nconst sol   = makeChar('Lucy',   'Sol',   10, 6);\nconst objects = engine.objectEngine.getAll();\n\n// Test 1: 'interact' redirect from bonsai \u2192 tea_set\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'interact', targetId: 'tea_set' }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test interact redirect:', JSON.stringify(events));\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\n// Test 2: 'interact' redirect to unavailable object (stove already full)\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('stove').reserve('Lucy'); // make stove full\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'interact', targetId: 'stove' }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test interact \u2192 full (unavailable):', JSON.stringify(events));\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('stove').release('Lucy');\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\n// Test 3: 'talk' redirect from bonsai \u2192 Sol\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'talk', targetCharacter: 'Sol' }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test talk redirect:', JSON.stringify(events));\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\n// Test 4: 'move' redirect from bonsai \u2192 tile\n{\n  const events = [];\n  const simState = { pushEvent: (ev) => events.push(ev), findAdjacentWalkable: engine._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(engine) };\n  maple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\n  engine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').reserve('Amelia');\n  ai._applyDecision(maple, { action: 'move', targetTile: { tx: 5, ty: 5 } }, [maple, sol], objects, simState);\n  console.log('Test move redirect:', JSON.stringify(events));\n  maple.targetObjectId = null;\n}\n\" 2>&1"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "node --input-type=module --eval \"\nimport { SimulationEngine } from './src/Engine.js';\nimport { Character } from './src/Character.js';\nimport { OBJECT_DEFS } from './src/objects.js';\n\nconst GRID_COLS = 19, GRID_ROWS = 13;\nconst walkable = Array.from({length: GRID_ROWS}, (_, row) =>\n  Array.from({length: GRID_COLS}, (_, col) => col >= 1 && col <= 17 && row >= 2 && row <= 9));\nfor (const def of OBJECT_DEFS) {\n  const {tx, ty} = def.position;\n  if (ty >= 0 && ty < GRID_ROWS && tx >= 0 && tx < GRID_COLS) walkable[ty][tx] = false;\n}\n\nconst engine = new SimulationEngine({ walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\nconst maple = new Character({ name: 'Amelia', label: 'Maple', tx: 6, ty: 7, walkable, gridCols: GRID_COLS, gridRows: GRID_ROWS });\nengine.addCharacter(maple);\n\n// Simulate: character arrives at bonsai, but bonsai is in 'tending' state (no use transition)\nmaple.targetObjectId = 'bonsai';\nmaple.pendingAction  = 'use';\nmaple.action         = 'idle';\n// Manually put bonsai into 'tending' state (simulating it changed while char was walking)\nengine.objectEngine.getById('bonsai').state = 'tending';\n\nengine.update(0);  // trigger _handleObjectArrival\n\nconsole.log('Fallback events from Engine.js path:', JSON.stringify(engine.pendingEvents));\n\" 2>&1"
}

    }

}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #107
The Engine.js fallback path still works correctly. When the object is in an incompatible state (no 'use' transition), `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` is called and emits a fallback event. The character redirects to the kotatsu (the closest available alternative). Now let me update the todo list and commit the changes.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #113
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git add src/AIEngine.js debug.html && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix: emit FALLBACK events when AI redirects characters mid-walk (FRG-227)\n\nRoot cause: _fallbackToAlternativeObject in Engine.js is almost never\ntriggered in normal sim operation because the slot reservation system\n(FRG-222) prevents characters from arriving at unavailable objects.\nThe visible \"redirects\" QA observed come from AIEngine._applyDecision\noverriding a character's object target, but no fallback events were\nemitted there.\n\nFix: emit 'fallback' pendingEvents via simState.pushEvent in all four\ncases of _applyDecision that can abandon an in-progress object target:\n- 'interact': redirect to a different object \u2192 reason 'plan_changed'\n- 'interact': new target unavailable (full) \u2192 reason 'object_unavailable'\n- 'idle': AI parks character, abandoning object walk \u2192 'plan_changed'\n- 'move': AI sends to free tile, abandoning object walk \u2192 'plan_changed'\n- 'talk': AI sends to talk, abandoning object walk \u2192 'plan_changed'\n\nAlso add 'plan_changed' rendering in debug.html event log so the new\nreason string displays as human-readable \"plan changed\".\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01AApKtNWczfZfSNfH81qj1r",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "git add src/AIEngine.js debug.html && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix: emit FALLBACK events when AI redirects characters mid-walk (FRG-227)\n\nRoot cause: _fallbackToAlternativeObject in Engine.js is almost never\ntriggered in normal sim operation because the slot reservation system\n(FRG-222) prevents characters from arriving at unavailable objects.\nThe visible \"redirects\" QA observed come from AIEngine._applyDecision\noverriding a character's object target, but no fallback events were\nemitted there.\n\nFix: emit 'fallback' pendingEvents via simState.pushEvent in all four\ncases of _applyDecision that can abandon an in-progress object target:\n- 'interact': redirect to a different object \u2192 reason 'plan_changed'\n- 'interact': new target unavailable (full) \u2192 reason 'object_unavailable'\n- 'idle': AI parks character, abandoning object walk \u2192 'plan_changed'\n- 'move': AI sends to free tile, abandoning object walk \u2192 'plan_changed'\n- 'talk': AI sends to talk, abandoning object walk \u2192 'plan_changed'\n\nAlso add 'plan_changed' rendering in debug.html event log so the new\nreason string displays as human-readable \"plan changed\".\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #117
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "gh pr create --title \"fix: emit FALLBACK events when AI redirects characters mid-walk (FRG-227)\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n\n- **Root cause**: `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` in `Engine.js` is almost never triggered in normal sim operation because the slot reservation system (FRG-222) prevents characters from arriving at unavailable objects. The visible \"redirects\" QA observed come from `AIEngine._applyDecision` overriding a character's object target mid-walk \u2014 but no `'fallback'` events were emitted from that path.\n- **Fix in `src/AIEngine.js`**: Added `simState.pushEvent` calls in all four `_applyDecision` cases that can abandon an in-progress object target (`interact`, `idle`, `move`, `talk`). Uses reason `'plan_changed'` when the AI voluntarily redirects, and `'object_unavailable'` when the new target is also full.\n- **Fix in `debug.html`**: Added `'plan_changed'` \u2192 `'plan changed'` mapping to the fallback event renderer so the new reason string displays cleanly.\n\n## Test plan\n- [ ] Open `debug.html` and let the sim run \u2014 FALLBACK entries should now appear in the event log whenever a character's object target changes mid-walk due to an AI decision\n- [ ] Verify entries show correct char name, reason (`unavailable` / `plan changed` / `target moved`), and from/to object names\n- [ ] Verify that the existing Engine.js fallback path (manual debug-button state change while character is en route) still emits a FALLBACK event correctly\n- [ ] Verify no regressions: object reservations are still properly released in all redirect cases\n\n\ud83e\udd16 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\""
}

The visible \"redirects\" QA observed come from `AIEngine._applyDecision` overriding a character's object target mid-walk \u2014 but no `'fallback'` events were emitted from that path.\n- **Fix in `src/AIEngine.js`**: Added `simState.pushEvent` calls in all four `_applyDecision` cases that can abandon an in-progress object target (`interact`, `idle`, `move`, `talk`). Uses reason `'plan_changed'` when the AI voluntarily redirects, and `'object_unavailable'` when the new target is also full.\n- **Fix in `debug.html`**: Added `'plan_changed'` \u2192 `'plan changed'` mapping to the fallback event renderer so the new reason string displays cleanly.\n\n## Test plan\n- [ ] Open `debug.html` and let the sim run \u2014 FALLBACK entries should now appear in the event log whenever a character's object target changes mid-walk due to an AI decision\n- [ ] Verify entries show correct char name, reason (`unavailable` / `plan changed` / `target moved`), and from/to object names\n- [ ] Verify that the existing Engine.js fallback path (manual debug-button state change while character is en route) still emits a FALLBACK event correctly\n- [ ] Verify no regressions: object reservations are still properly released in all redirect cases\n\n\ud83e\udd16 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\""
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #120
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
{"id":"2f29bfac-e5c2-4808-8ba2-a1fe32b68d95","identifier":"FRG-227","title":"Bug: FALLBACK events never appear in debug event log","description":"## Problem

QA ran 14+ sim hours across two sessions. Characters DO redirect when targets are full (behavior works), but no FALLBACK event ever appears in the debug event log. The fallback event emission or consumption in debug.html is broken.

## Evidence

* Zero FALLBACK entries in 14+ hours of observation
* Characters visibly redirect (e.g., Nyx targets bonsai while Maple is using it, Nyx walks elsewhere)
* LLM even acknowledges conflicts in dialogue (Maple: 'it looks like Sol is already using the tea set')
* The code in Engine.js pushes fallback events via pendingEvents, and debug.html has CSS + rendering for them

## Investigation

1. Check if \\_fallbackToAlternativeObject is actually being called (add console.log)
2. Check if pendingEvents consumption in debug.html tick loop handles fallback events correctly
3. Verify the event type string matches between push and render

## Acceptance Criteria

* FALLBACK events visible in debug event log when character redirects
* Events show reason and from/to objects","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-227/bug-fallback-events-never-appear-in-debug-event-log","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-227-bug-fallback-events-never-appear-in-debug-event-log","createdAt":"2026-03-07T21:18:40.598Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T21:43:44.323Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"1b965b61-9e63-41f4-8e68-55cd3bdad4fa","title":"fix: emit FALLBACK events when AI redirects characters mid-walk (FRG-227)","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/11"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Chill","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #123
## Status DONE ## What Changed - **`src/AIEngine.js`**: Added `simState.pushEvent` calls in all four `_applyDecision` cases that abandon an in-progress object target. `'interact'` emits `plan_changed` (redirect to new object) or `object_unavailable` (new target was also full → idle). `'idle'`, `'move'`, `'talk'` emit `plan_changed` with the abandoned object as `fromLabel` and the new destination (or null for idle) as `toLabel`. - **`debug.html`**: Added `'plan_changed'` → `'plan changed'` case to the fallback event renderer. ## Key Decisions - **Root cause is not a rendering bug**: `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` in `Engine.js` is almost never triggered in practice because the FRG-222 slot reservation system prevents characters from arriving at unavailable objects. The "redirects" QA observed come from `AIEngine._applyDecision` overriding a character's target mid-walk. - Used `'plan_changed'` as a new reason code (distinct from `'object_unavailable'`) to distinguish AI-driven redirects from arrival-time failures. - The existing Engine.js fallback path (triggered when object state changes while char is en route) still works and emits its own events unchanged. ## PR / Branch - Branch: `feature/frg-227-fix-fallback-events` - PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/11
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- **`src/AIEngine.js`**: Added `simState.pushEvent` calls in all four `_applyDecision` cases that abandon an in-progress object target. `'interact'` emits `plan_changed` (redirect to new object) or `object_unavailable` (new target was also full \u2192 idle). `'idle'`, `'move'`, `'talk'` emit `plan_changed` with the abandoned object as `fromLabel` and the new destination (or null for idle) as `toLabel`.\n- **`debug.html`**: Added `'plan_changed'` \u2192 `'plan changed'` case to the fallback event renderer.\n\n## Key Decisions\n- **Root cause is not a rendering bug**: `_fallbackToAlternativeObject` in `Engine.js` is almost never triggered in practice because the FRG-222 slot reservation system prevents characters from arriving at unavailable objects. The \"redirects\" QA observed come from `AIEngine._applyDecision` overriding a character's target mid-walk.\n- Used `'plan_changed'` as a new reason code (distinct from `'object_unavailable'`) to distinguish AI-driven redirects from arrival-time failures.\n- The existing Engine.js fallback path (triggered when object state changes while char is en route) still works and emits its own events unchanged.\n\n## PR / Branch\n- Branch: `feature/frg-227-fix-fallback-events`\n- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/11"
  }
}
